1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Kaf-Ha-Ya-A’in-Sad. (* Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah and to whomever He reveals know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Kaf- Ha-Ya-'Ain-Sad. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Commemorate the beneficence of your Lord on Zachariah, His devotee,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: This is the remembrance of the mercy of your Lord upon His bondman Zakaria.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: The mention of thy Lord's mercy unto His servant Zachariah;

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Mention of the mercy of thy Lord to His bondman Zakariyya.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: (This is) a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His slave Zakariya (Zachariah).

2.author: Itani
2.text: A mention of the mercy of your Lord towards His servant Zechariah.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: This is an account of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: A reminder of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zakariyya.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: A mention of the mercy of thy Lord unto His servant Zachariah.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: [This is] an account of your Lord’s mercy on His servant, Zechariah,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: A mention of the Mercy of your Lord to his worshiper Zachariah,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: [This is] a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: This is the story of the blessing of your Lord to His servant Zachariah.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: A mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zakariya.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: This is an account of your Lord's mercy bestowed upon His servant Zachariah,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: (This is) a recital of the Mercy of thy Lord to His servant Zakariya.

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: When he called to his Lord inwardly,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: When he softly prayed to his Lord.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: when he called upon his Lord secretly

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Recall what time he cried unto his Lord with a cry secret.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: When he called out his Lord (Allah) a call in secret,

3.author: Itani
3.text: When he called on his Lord, a call in seclusion.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: when he cried to his Lord in secret.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: When he called his Lord [with] a call in secret.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: When he cried unto his Lord a cry in secret,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: when he called out to his Lord with a secret cry.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: when he called upon his Lord in secret,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: When he called to his Lord a private supplication.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: When he quietly called his Lord

3.author: Shakir
3.text: When he called upon his Lord in a low voice,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: when he called upon his Lord in low tones, saying,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Behold! he cried to his Lord in secret,

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: And said: "O my Lord, my bones decay, my head is white and hoary, yet in calling You, O Lord, I have never been deprived.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: He submitted, “O my Lord – my bones have become weak and old age shines forth from my head, and O my Lord, I have never been disappointed in my prayer to you.”

4.author: Arberry
4.text: saying, 'O my Lord, behold the bones within me are feeble and my head is all aflame with hoariness. And in calling on Thee, my Lord, I have never been hitherto unprosperous.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: He said: O my Lord! verily the bones of me have waxen feeble, and the head is glistening with hoariness, and have not yet been in my prayer to thee, my Lord, unblest.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Saying: "My Lord! Indeed my bones have grown feeble, and grey hair has spread on my head, And I have never been unblest in my invocation to You, O my Lord!

4.author: Itani
4.text: He said, “My Lord, my bones have become feeble, and my hair is aflame with gray, and never, Lord, have I been disappointed in my prayer to you.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: He said: "Lord! My bones have grown feeble and my head is glistening with age; yet, never have my prayers to You, my Lord, been unfruitful.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: He said: "My Lord! Indeed my bones have grown feeble, and gray hair has Ashta`al on my head, and I have never been unblessed in my invocation to You, O my Lord!"

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Saying: My Lord! Lo! the bones of me wax feeble and my head is shining with grey hair, and I have never been unblest in prayer to Thee, my Lord.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed my bones have become feeble, and my head has turned white with age, yet never have I, my Lord, been disappointed in supplicating You!

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: saying: 'O my Lord, my bones are enfeebled, and my head glows silver with age. Yet, never Lord, have I been unblessed in prayer to You.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: He said, "My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white, and never have I been in my supplication to You, my Lord, unhappy.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: and said, "My Lord, my bones have become feeble and my hair has turned white with age. Yet I have never been deprived in receiving from You the answer to my prayers.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: He said: My Lord! surely my bones are weakened and my head flares with hoariness, and, my Lord! I have never been unsuccessful in my prayer to Thee:

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: "Lord, my bones have weakened and my head has turned hoary with age, but never, Lord, have I been disappointed in my prayer to you:

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Praying: "O my Lord! infirm indeed are my bones, and the hair of my head doth glisten with grey: but never am I unblest, O my Lord, in my prayer to Thee!

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Qala rabbi innee wahana alAAathmu minnee waishtaAAala alrrasu shayban walam akun biduAAaika rabbi shaqiyyan

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: But I fear my relatives after me; and my wife is barren. So grant me a successor as a favour from You

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: “And I fear my relatives after me and my wife is barren therefore bestow upon me from Yourself one who will take up my work.”

5.author: Arberry
5.text: And now I fear my kinsfolk after I am gone; and my wife is barren. So give me, from Thee, a kinsman

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And verily I fear my kindred after me, and my wife hath been barren; so bestow on me from before Thee an heir.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: "And Verily! I fear my relatives after me, since my wife is barren. So give me from Yourself an heir,

5.author: Itani
5.text: “And I fear for my dependents after me, and my wife is barren. So grant me, from Yourself, an heir.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: I fear evil from my kinsmen after I am gone; and my wife is barren, so grant me an heir out of Your special grace,

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: "And verily, I fear Mawali after me, and my wife is barren. So give me from Yourself an heir."

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Lo! I fear my kinsfolk after me, since my wife is barren. Oh, give me from Thy presence a successor

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Indeed I fear my kinsmen, after me, and my wife is barren. So grant me from Yourself an heir

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Indeed, I fear my kinsmen who will succeed me, for my wife is barren. Grant me a kinsman

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And indeed, I fear the successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so give me from Yourself an heir

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: I am afraid of what my kinsmen will do after (my death) and my wife is barren. Lord, grant me a son

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And surely I fear my cousins after me, and my wife is barren, therefore grant me from Thyself an heir,

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: now I fear my kinsmen when I am gone. [I have no hope of their continuing my mission] for my wife is barren, so grant me a successor from Yourself,

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: "Now I fear (what) my relatives (and colleagues) (will do) after me: but my wife is barren: so give me an heir as from Thyself,-

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Wainnee khiftu almawaliya min waraee wakanati imraatee AAaqiran fahab lee min ladunka waliyyan

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Who will be heir to me, and heir to the house of Jacob; and make him obedient to You, O Lord."

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: “He being my successor and the heir of the Descendants of Yaqub (Jacob); and my Lord, make him a cherished* one.” (* Make him a Prophet among the Descendants of Israel.)

6.author: Arberry
6.text: who shall be my inheritor and the inheritor of the House of Jacob; and make him, my Lord, well-pleasing.'

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Inheriting me and inheriting the Children of Ya'qub; and make him, my Lord, acceptable!

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: "Who shall inherit me, and inherit (also) the posterity of Ya'qub (Jacob) (inheritance of the religious knowledge and Prophethood, not the wealth, etc.). And make him, my Lord, one with whom You are Well-pleased!".

6.author: Itani
6.text: To inherit me, and inherit from the House of Jacob, and make him, my Lord, pleasing.”

6.author: Maududi
6.text: one that might be my heir and the heir of the house of Jacob; and make him, Lord, one that will be pleasing to You.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: "Who shall inherit me, and inherit (also) the posterity of Ya`qub. And make him, my Lord, one with whom You are well-pleased!"

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Who shall inherit of me and inherit (also) of the house of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, acceptable (unto Thee).

6.author: Qarai
6.text: who may inherit from me and inherit from the House of Jacob, and make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]!’

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: who will be my heir and an heir to the House of Jacob, and make him, my Lord, satisfied'

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]."

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: who will be my heir and the heir of the family of Jacob. Lord, make him a person who will please you".

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Who should inherit me and inherit from the children of Yaqoub, and make him, my Lord, one in whom Thou art well pleased.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: to be my heir and to be the heir [of the blessings] of the House of Jacob; and make him, O my Lord, acceptable to you."

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: "(One that) will (truly) represent me, and represent the posterity of Jacob; and make him, O my Lord! one with whom Thou art well-pleased!"

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Yarithunee wayarithu min ali yaAAqooba waijAAalhu rabbi radiyyan

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: "O Zachariah," (it was) said, "We give you good news of a son by name of John.' To none have We attributed the name before."

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: “O Zakaria! We give you the glad tidings of a son whose name is Yahya (John) – before him, We have not created anyone of this name.”

7.author: Arberry
7.text: 'O Zachariah, We give thee good tidings of a boy, whose name is John. No namesake have We given him aforetime.'

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: O Zakariyya! verily We give thee the glad tidings of a boy: his name shall be Yahya; We have not afore-time made his namesake.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: (Allah said) "O Zakariya (Zachariah)! Verily, We give you the glad tidings of a son, His name will be Yahya (John). We have given that name to none before (him)."

7.author: Itani
7.text: “O Zechariah, We give you good news of a son, whose name is John, a name We have never given before.”

7.author: Maududi
7.text: (He was told): "Zechariah, We bring you the good news of the birth of a son whose name shall be Yahya (John), one whose namesake We never created before."

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: (Allah said:) "O Zakariyya! Verily, We give you the good news of a son, whose name will be Yahya. We have given that name to none before (him)."

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: (It was said unto him): O Zachariah! Lo! We bring thee tidings of a son whose name is John; we have given the same name to none before (him).

7.author: Qarai
7.text: ‘O Zechariah! Indeed We give you the good news of a son, whose name is ‘‘John.’’ Never before have We made anyone his namesake.’

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: 'O Zachariah, We give you good tidings of a son, and he shall be called John (Yahya); a name We have never given before'

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: [He was told], "O Zechariah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be John. We have not assigned to any before [this] name."

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: We answered his prayers with the glad news of the birth of a son by the name of John and told him, "We have never given such a name to anyone else."

7.author: Shakir
7.text: O Zakariya! surely We give you good news of a boy whose name shall be Yahya: We have not made before anyone his equal.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: "Zachariah, We bring you good news of a son whose name shall be John. We have not given such a name to anyone before."

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: (His prayer was answered): "O Zakariya! We give thee good news of a son: His name shall be Yahya: on none by that name have We conferred distinction before."

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Ya zakariyya inna nubashshiruka bighulamin ismuhu yahya lam najAAal lahu min qablu samiyyan

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: "How can I have a son, O Lord" he said, "when my wife is barren and I am old and decrepit?"

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: He submitted, “My Lord – how can I have a son whereas my wife is barren and I have reached infirmity due to old age?”

8.author: Arberry
8.text: He said, 'O my Lord, how shall I have a son, seeing my wife is barren, and I have attained to the declining of old age?'

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: He said: O my Lord! in what wise shall there be for me a boy, whereas my wife hath been barren, and surely have reached an age of extreme degree?

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: He said: "My Lord! How can I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I have reached the extreme old age."

8.author: Itani
8.text: He said, “My Lord, how can I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I have become decrepit with old age?”

8.author: Maududi
8.text: He said: "My Lord! How can I have a boy when my wife is barren and I have reached an extremely old age?"

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: He said: "My Lord! How can I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I have reached the extreme old age."

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: He said: My Lord! How can I have a son when my wife is barren and I have reached infirm old age?

8.author: Qarai
8.text: He said, ‘My Lord! How shall I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I am already advanced in age?’

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: 'How shall I have a son, Lord' he asked, 'when my wife is barren, and I am advanced in years'

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: He said, "My Lord, how will I have a boy when my wife has been barren and I have reached extreme old age?"

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: He said, "Lord, how can I have a son? My wife is barren and I have reached an extremely old age".

8.author: Shakir
8.text: He said: O my Lord! when shall I have a son, and my wife is barren, and I myself have reached indeed the extreme degree of old age?

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: "My Lord!" [said Zachariah],  "How shall I have a son when my wife is barren and I have reached such extreme old age?"

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: He said: "O my Lord! How shall I have a son, when my wife is barren and I have grown quite decrepit from old age?"

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Qala rabbi anna yakoonu lee ghulamun wakanati imraatee AAaqiran waqad balaghtu mina alkibari AAitiyyan

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: (The angel) answered: "Thus will it be. Your Lord said: 'This is easy for Me; for when I brought you into being you were nothing.'

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: He (the angel) said, “So it is; your Lord says, ‘This is easy for Me – in fact I created you before this, at a time when you did not exist.’”

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Said He, 'So it shall be; thy Lord says, "Easy is that for Me, seeing that I created thee aforetime, when thou wast nothing."'

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Allah said: even so! Thy Lord saith: it is unto Me easy, whereas surely I created thee afore when thou wast not aught.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: He said: "So (it will be). Your Lord says; It is easy for Me. Certainly I have created you before, when you had been nothing!"

9.author: Itani
9.text: He said, “It will be so, your Lord says, ‘it is easy for me, and I created you before, when you were nothing.’”

9.author: Maududi
9.text: He answered: "So shall it be." Your Lord says: "It is easy for Me," and then added: "For beyond doubt, I created you earlier when you were nothing."

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: He said: "Thus your Lord says: `It is easy for Me. Certainly I have created you before, when you had been nothing!"') [19:8-9]

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: He said: So (it will be). Thy Lord saith: It is easy for Me, even as I created thee before, when thou wast naught.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: He said, ‘So shall it be. Your Lord has said, ‘‘It is simple for Me.’’ Certainly I created you before when you were nothing.’

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: He replied: 'It shall be so; your Lord says: "It is easy for Me, indeed, I created you before time when you were not a thing."

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: [An angel] said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, for I created you before, while you were nothing.' "

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: (The angel) said, "This is true, but your Lord says, 'For Me it is easy; I created you when you did not exist".

9.author: Shakir
9.text: He said: So shall it be, your Lord says: It is easy to Me, and indeed I created you before, when you were nothing.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: He said, "It will be so! Your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me for I created you when you were nothing before.'"

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: He said: "So (it will be) thy Lord saith, 'that is easy for Me: I did indeed create thee before, when thou hadst been nothing!'"

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Qala kathalika qala rabbuka huwa AAalayya hayyinun waqad khalaqtuka min qablu walam taku shayan

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: He said: "O Lord, give me a token." "Though sound," He answered, "you will not talk to any one for three nights running."

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: He said, “My Lord, give me a sign”; He said, “Your token is that you will not speak to people for three nights, although in proper health.”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: He said, 'Lord, appoint to me some sign.' Said He, 'Thy sign is that thou shall not speak to men, though being without fault, three nights.'

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: He said: my Lord appoint for me a sign. Allah said: thy sign is that thou shalt not speak unto mankind for three nights, while sound.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: [Zakariya (Zachariah)] said: "My Lord! Appoint for me a sign." He said: "Your sign is that you shall not speak unto mankind for three nights, though having no bodily defect."

10.author: Itani
10.text: He said, “My Lord, give me a sign.” He said, “Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights straight.”

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Zechariah said: "Lord, grant me a Sign." Said He: "Your Sign is that you shall not be able to speak to people for three nights, though you will be otherwise sound."

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: He [Zakariyya] said: "My Lord! Appoint for me a sign." He said: "Your sign is that you shall not speak unto mankind for three nights, though having no bodily defect."

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: He said: My Lord! Appoint for me some token. He said: Thy token is that thou, with no bodily defect, shalt not speak unto mankind three nights.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Appoint a sign for me.’ He said, ‘Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three complete nights.’

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: He (Zachariah) said: 'Lord, make for me a sign' He replied: 'Your sign is that you shall not speak to people for three nights being without fault'

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound."

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Zachariah asked, " Lord, show me evidence (if this is a heavenly news)." The Lord said, "The evidence for it is that you must not speak (to any mortal) though you are in good health for three nights (and days)."

10.author: Shakir
10.text: He said: My Lord! give me a sign. He said: Your sign is that you will not be able to speak to the people three nights while in sound health.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: He said, "My Lord, grant me a sign!" He said, "Your sign will be that you will not speak to anyone for three successive days and nights, although sound in body."

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: (Zakariya) said: "O my Lord! give me a Sign." "Thy Sign," was the answer, "Shall be that thou shalt speak to no man for three nights, although thou art not dumb."

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Qala rabbi ijAAal lee ayatan qala ayatuka alla tukallima alnnasa thalatha layalin sawiyyan

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: So he came from the chamber to his people, and suggested to them (by signs) to sing the praises of the Lord morning and evening.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: He therefore emerged upon his people from the mosque, and told them through gestures, “Keep proclaiming the Purity (of your Lord) morning and evening.”

11.author: Arberry
11.text: So he came forth unto his people from the Sanctuary, then he made signal to them, 'Give you glory at dawn and evening.'

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Then he came forth to his people from the sanctuary, and he beckoned unto them. hallow your Lord morning and evening.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Then he came out to his people from Al-Mihrab (a praying place or a private room, etc.), he told them by signs to glorify Allah's Praises in the morning and in the afternoon.

11.author: Itani
11.text: And he came out to his people, from the sanctuary, and signaled to them to praise morning and evening.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Thereupon Zechariah came out from the sanctuary and directed his people by gestures to extol His glory by day and by night.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Then he came out to his people from the Mihrab and he indicated to them by signs to glorify [Allah] in the morning and in the afternoon.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Then he came forth unto his people from the sanctuary, and signified to them: Glorify your Lord at break of day and fall of night.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: So he emerged before his people from the Temple, and signalled to them that they should glorify [Allah] morning and evening.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Then he came out from the Sanctuary to his nation, and gestured to them to exalt (their Lord) at the dawn and at the evening.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: So he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt [Allah] in the morning and afternoon.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: Zachariah came out to his people from place of worship and inspired them to glorify the Lord both in the morning and evening.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: So he went forth to his people from his place of worship, then he made known to them that they should glorify (Allah) morning and evening.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Then Zachariah came forth from the shrine to his people and told them by signs to glorify the Lord morning and evening.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: So Zakariya came out to his people from him chamber: He told them by signs to celebrate Allah's praises in the morning and in the evening.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Fakharaja AAala qawmihi mina almihrabi faawha ilayhim an sabbihoo bukratan waAAashiyyan

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: (We said:) "O John, hold fast to the Book;" and We gave him wisdom right from boyhood,

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: “O Yahya – hold the Book firmly”; and We gave him Prophethood in his infancy. (Prophet Yahya was only 2 years old at that time.)

12.author: Arberry
12.text: 'O John, take the Book forcefully'; and We gave him judgment, yet a little child,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: O Yahya! se hold fast the book. And We vouchsafed unto him wisdom, while yet a child.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: (It was said to his son): "O Yahya (John)! Hold fast the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah)]." And We gave him wisdom while yet a child.

12.author: Itani
12.text: “O John, hold on to the Scripture firmly,” and We gave him wisdom in his youth.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: "O John! Hold the Book with all your strength." We had bestowed wisdom upon him while he was still a child;

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: (It was said to his son): "O Yahya! Hold fast to the Scripture." And We gave him wisdom while yet a child.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: (And it was said unto his son): O John! Hold fast the Scripture. And we gave him wisdom when a child,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: ‘O John!’ [We said,] ‘Hold on with power to the Book!’ And We gave him judgement while still a child,

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: (We said): 'O John, hold fast to the Book', and We bestowed on him judgment while yet a child

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: [Allah] said, "O John, take the Scripture with determination." And We gave him judgement [while yet] a boy

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: We commanded John, Zachariah's son, to follow the guidance of the Lord with due steadfastness. To John We gave knowledge and wisdom to John during his childhood.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: O Yahya! take hold of the Book with strength, and We granted him wisdom while yet a child

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: To John We said, "Hold fast the Book," and while he was still a child, We bestowed upon him wisdom,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: (To his son came the command): "O Yahya! take hold of the Book with might": and We gave him Wisdom even as a youth,

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Ya yahya khuthi alkitaba biquwwatin waataynahu alhukma sabiyyan

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And compassion from Us, and goodness. So he was devout,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And compassion from Ourselves, and chastity; and he was extremely pious.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and a tenderness from Us, and purity; and he was godfearing,

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And tenderness from Our presence and purity, and he was God-fearing.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And (made him) sympathetic to men as a mercy (or a grant) from Us, and pure from sins [i.e. Yahya (John)] and he was righteous,

13.author: Itani
13.text: And tenderness from Us, and innocence. He was devout.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and We also endowed him with tenderness and purity, and he was exceedingly pious

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And Hananan from Us, and Zakatan, and he was pious,

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And compassion from Our presence, and purity; and he was devout,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and a compassion and purity from Us. He was Godwary

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and tenderness from Us and purity, and he was cautious;

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And affection from Us and purity, and he was fearing of Allah

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: We gave him compassion and purity. He was a pious human being,

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And tenderness from Us and purity, and he was one who guarded (against evil),

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and tenderness [of heart] and purity. He was pious,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And piety (for all creatures) as from Us, and purity: He was devout,

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Wahananan min ladunna wazakatan wakana taqiyyan

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And kind to his parents, neither arrogant nor disobedient.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And was good to his parents and not forceful, nor disobedient.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and cherishing his parents, not arrogant, rebellious.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And duteous unto his parents, and Was not high-handed rebel.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And dutiful towards his parents, and he was neither an arrogant nor disobedient (to Allah or to his parents).

14.author: Itani
14.text: And kind to his parents; and he was not a disobedient tyrant.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and cherishing to his parents. Never was he insolent or rebellious.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not arrogant or disobedient.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And dutiful toward his parents. And he was not arrogant, rebellious.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and good to his parents, and was not self-willed or disobedient.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: honoring his parents, being neither arrogant nor rebellious.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not a disobedient tyrant.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: kind to his parents, not arrogant or a rebellious person.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not insolent, disobedient.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and dutiful towards his parents and was not haughty or disobedient.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And kind to his parents, and he was not overbearing or rebellious.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Wabarran biwalidayhi walam yakun jabbaran AAasiyyan

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: So peace on him the day he was born, the day he will die, and the day that he will be raised from the dead.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And peace is upon him the day he was born, and the day he will taste death, and the day he will be raised alive.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: 'Peace be upon him, the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he is raised up alive!'

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And Peace be unto him the day he was born and the day he dieth and the day he will be raised up alive.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: So Peace on him the day he was born the day that he dies and the day that he will be raised up to life (again)!

15.author: Itani
15.text: And peace be upon him the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the Day he is raised alive.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Peace be upon him the day he was born, and the day he will die, and the day he will be raised up alive.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And Salam (peace) be on him the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he will be raised up to life (again)!

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Peace on him the day he was born, and the day he dieth and the day he shall be raised alive!

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Peace be to him, the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive!

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Peace be upon him on the day he was born and the day he dies, and on him the day when he is raised up alive.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And peace be upon him the day he was born and the day he dies and the day he is raised alive.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: He was born and died in peace and will be brought back to life again in peace.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And peace on him on the day he was born, and on the day he dies, and on the day he is raised to life

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Peace be on him on the day of his birth, and on the day of his death, and peace will be on him on the day he is raised up to life again.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: So Peace on him the day he was born, the day that he dies, and the day that he will be raised up to life (again)!

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Wasalamun AAalayhi yawma wulida wayawma yamootu wayawma yubAAathu hayyan

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Commemorate Mary in the Book. When she withdrew from her family to a place in the East

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And remember Maryam in the Book; when she went away from her family to a place towards east.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: And mention in the Book Mary when she withdrew from her people to an eastern place,

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And mention thou in the Book Maryam, what time the retired from her people to a place eastward.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And mention in the Book (the Quran, O Muhammad SAW, the story of) Maryam (Mary), when she withdrew in seclusion from her family to a place facing east.

16.author: Itani
16.text: And mention in the Scripture Mary, when she withdrew from her people to an eastern location.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: (O Muhammad), recite in the Book the account of Mary, when she withdrew from her people to a place towards the east;

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And mention in the Book, Maryam, when she withdrew in seclusion from her family to place facing east.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And make mention of Mary in the Scripture, when she had withdrawn from her people to a chamber looking East,

16.author: Qarai
16.text: And mention in the Book Mary, when she withdrew from her family to an easterly place.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: And mention in the Book, Mary, how she withdrew from her people to an eastern place and she took a veil apart from them;

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And mention, [O Muhammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: (Muhammad), mention in the Book (the Quran) the story of Mary how she left her family and started living in a solitary place to the East

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And mention Marium in the Book when she drew aside from her family to an eastern place;

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Recount in the Book how Mary withdrew from her people to an eastern place

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Relate in the Book (the story of) Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place in the East.

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Waothkur fee alkitabi maryama ithi intabathat min ahliha makanan sharqiyyan

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: And took cover from them, We sent a spirit of Ours to her who appeared before her in the concrete form of a man.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: So there she screened herself from them; We therefore sent Our Spirit towards her – he appeared before her in the form of a healthy man. (Angel Jibreel – peace be upon him.)

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and she took a veil apart from them; then We sent unto her Our Spirit that presented himself to her a man without fault.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Then she took beside them a curtain; then We sent unto her Our Spirit, and he took unto her the form of a human being sound.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: She placed a screen (to screen herself) from them; then We sent to her Our Ruh [angel Jibrael (Gabriel)], and he appeared before her in the form of a man in all respects.

17.author: Itani
17.text: She screened herself away from them, and We sent to her Our spirit, and He appeared to her as an immaculate human.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: and drew a curtain, screening herself from people whereupon We sent to her Our spirit and he appeared to her as a well-shaped man.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: She placed a screen before them; then We sent to her Our Ruh, and he appeared before her in the form of a man in all respects.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And had chosen seclusion from them. Then We sent unto her Our Spirit and it assumed for her the likeness of a perfect man.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Thus did she seclude herself from them, whereupon We sent to her Our Spirit and he became incarnate for her as a well-proportioned human.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: We sent to her Our Spirit (Gabriel) in the resemblance of a perfect human.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And she took, in seclusion from them, a screen. Then We sent to her Our Angel, and he represented himself to her as a well-proportioned man.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: out of her people's sight. We sent Our Spirit to her, who stood before her in the shape of a well formed human being.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: So she took a veil (to screen herself) from them; then We sent to her Our spirit, and there appeared to her a well-made man.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and kept herself in seclusion from them. We sent her Our angel, who presented himself to her as a full-grown human being.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: She placed a screen (to screen herself) from them; then We sent her our angel, and he appeared before her as a man in all respects.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Faittakhathat min doonihim hijaban faarsalna ilayha roohana fatamaththala laha basharan sawiyyan

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: "I seek refuge in the Merciful from you, if you fear Him," she said.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: She said, “I seek the refuge of the Most Gracious from you – if you fear God.”

18.author: Arberry
18.text: She said, 'I take refuge in the All-merciful from thee!

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: She said: verily seek refuge with the Compassionate from thee if thou art God-fearing.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: She said: "Verily! I seek refuge with the Most Beneficent (Allah) from you, if you do fear Allah."

18.author: Itani
18.text: She said, “I take refuge from you in the Most Merciful, should you be righteous.”

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Mary exclaimed: "I surely take refuge from you with the Most Compassionate Lord, if you are at all God-fearing."

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: She said: "Verily, I seek refuge with the Most Gracious (Allah) from you, if you do fear Allah."

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: She said: Lo! I seek refuge in the Beneficent One from thee, if thou art Allah-fearing.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: She said, ‘I seek the protection of the All-beneficent from you, should you be Godwary!’

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: (And when she saw him) she said: 'I take refuge in the Merciful from you! If you are fearful'

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: She said, "Indeed, I seek refuge in the Most Merciful from you, [so leave me], if you should be fearing of Allah."

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: Mary said, "Would that the Beneficent God would protect me from you. Leave me alone if you are a God fearing person".

18.author: Shakir
18.text: She said: Surely I fly for refuge from you to the Beneficent Allah, if you are one guarding (against evil).

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: When she saw him, she said, "I seek refuge in the compassionate God from you; [do not come near] if you fear the Lord."

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: She said: "I seek refuge from thee to (Allah) Most Gracious: (come not near) if thou dost fear Allah."

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Qalat innee aAAoothu bialrrahmani minka in kunta taqiyyan

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: He replied: "I am only a messenger from your Lord (sent) to bestow a good son on you."

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: He said, “I am indeed one sent by your Lord; so that I may give you a chaste son.”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: If thou fearest God... He said, 'I am but a messenger come from thy Lord, to give thee a boy most pure.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: He said: I am but a meenger from thy Lord that I may bestow on thee a boy pure.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: (The angel) said: "I am only a Messenger from your Lord, (to announce) to you the gift of a righteous son."

19.author: Itani
19.text: He said, “I am only the messenger of your Lord, to give you the gift of a pure son.”

19.author: Maududi
19.text: He said: "I am just a message-bearer of your Lord, I have come to grant you a most pure boy."

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: (The angel) said: "I am only a messenger from your Lord, (to announce) to you the gift of a righteous son."

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: He said: I am only a messenger of thy Lord, that I may bestow on thee a faultless son.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: He said, ‘I am only a messenger of your Lord that I may give you a pure son.’

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: 'I am the Messenger of your Lord' he replied, 'and have come to give you a pure boy'

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: He said, "I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you [news of] a pure boy."

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: He said, "I am the Messengers of your Lord. I have come to give you a purified son".

19.author: Shakir
19.text: He said: I am only a messenger of your Lord: That I will give you a pure boy.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: "I am only the messenger of your Lord," he replied. "I shall bestow upon you the gift of a son endowed with purity."

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: He said: "Nay, I am only a messenger from thy Lord, (to announce) to thee the gift of a holy son.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Qala innama ana rasoolu rabbiki liahaba laki ghulaman zakiyyan

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: "How can I have a son," she said, "when no man has touched me, nor am I sinful?"

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: She said, “How can I bear a son? No man has ever touched me, nor am I of poor conduct!”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: She said, 'How shall I have a son whom no mortal has touched, neither have I been unchaste?'

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: She said: in what wise shall there be a boy unto me, whereas not a human being hath touched me, nor have I been a harlot?

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: She said: "How can I have a son, when no man has touched me, nor am I unchaste?"

20.author: Itani
20.text: She said, “How can I have a son, when no man has touched me, and I was never unchaste?”

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Mary said: "How can a boy be born to me when no man has even touched me, nor have I ever been unchaste?"

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: She said: "How can I have a son when no man has touched me, nor am I Baghiyya"

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: She said: How can I have a son when no mortal hath touched me, neither have I been unchaste?

20.author: Qarai
20.text: She said, ‘How shall I have a child seeing that no human being has ever touched me, nor have I been unchaste?’

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: 'How shall I bear a son' she answered, 'when I am not touched by a human and not unchaste'

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste?"

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: She said, "How can I have a son when no mortal has touched me nor am I an unchaste woman".

20.author: Shakir
20.text: She said: When shall I have a boy and no mortal has yet touched me, nor have I been unchaste?

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: She said, "How can I have a son when no man has touched me; and neither have I been unchaste?"

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: She said: "How shall I have a son, seeing that no man has touched me, and I am not unchaste?"

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Qalat anna yakoonu lee ghulamun walam yamsasnee basharun walam aku baghiyyan

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: He said: "Thus will it be. Your Lord said: 'It is easy for Me,' and that: 'We shall make him a sign for men and a blessing from Us.' This is a thing already decreed."

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: He said, “So it is; your Lord has said, ‘This is easy for Me’; and in order that We make him a sign for mankind and a Mercy from Us; and this matter has been decreed.”

21.author: Arberry
21.text: He said, 'Even so thy Lord has said: "Easy is that for Me; and that We may appoint him a sign unto men and a mercy from Us; it is a thing decreed."'

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: He said: even so! Thy Lord saith: it is With Me easy, and it is in order that We may make him a sign unto mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is an affair decreed.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: He said: "So (it will be), your Lord said: 'That is easy for Me (Allah): And (We wish) to appoint him as a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us (Allah), and it is a matter (already) decreed, (by Allah). 

21.author: Itani
21.text: He said, “Thus said your Lord, `It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign for humanity, and a mercy from Us. It is a matter already decided.'“

21.author: Maududi
21.text: The angel said: "Thus shall it be. Your Lord says: 'It is easy for Me; and We shall do so in order to make him a Sign for mankind and a mercy from Us. This has been decreed.' "

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: He said: "Thus says your Lord: `That is easy for Me (Allah). And (We wish) to appoint him as a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us (Allah), and it is a matter (already) decreed (by Allah)."'

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: He said: So (it will be). Thy Lord saith: It is easy for Me. And (it will be) that We may make of him a revelation for mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is a thing ordained.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: He said, ‘So shall it be. Your Lord says, ‘‘It is simple for Me.’’ And so that We may make him a sign for mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is a matter [already] decided.’

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: "Even so" he replied, "as such your Lord has said: 'Easy it is for Me. And We shall make him a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us. It is a matter decreed.'"

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: He said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter [already] decreed.' "

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: He said, "This is true but your Lord says, "It is very easy for Me. We have decided to give you a son as evidence (of Our existence) for human beings and a mercy from Us. This is a decree already ordained."

21.author: Shakir
21.text: He said: Even so; your Lord says: It is easy to Me: and that We may make him a sign to men and a mercy from Us, and it is a matter which has been decreed.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: [The angel] replied, "So shall it be; your Lord says, 'This is easy for Me; and We shall make him a sign to people and a blessing, from Us. This has been decreed.'"

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: He said: "So (it will be): Thy Lord saith, 'that is easy for Me: and (We wish) to appoint him as a Sign unto men and a Mercy from Us': It is a matter (so) decreed."

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Qala kathaliki qala rabbuki huwa AAalayya hayyinun walinajAAalahu ayatan lilnnasi warahmatan minna wakana amran maqdiyyan

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: When she conceived him she went away to a distant place.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: So she conceived him, and she went away with him to a far place.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: So she conceived him, and withdrew with him to a distant place.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: hen she conceived him, and she retired with him to a place far-off.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a far place (i.e. Bethlehem valley about 4-6 miles from Jerusalem).

22.author: Itani
22.text: So she carried him, and secluded herself with him in a remote place.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Then she conceived him and withdrew with him to a far-off place.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a far place.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Thus she conceived him, then withdrew with him to a distant place.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Thereupon she carried him, and retired to a far off place.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: She conceived the child and retreated with him to a distant and solitary place.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: So she conceived him; then withdrew herself with him to a remote place.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a distant place.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: So she conceived him, and she retired with him to a remote place.

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: The birth pangs led her to the trunk of a date-palm tree. "Would that I had died before this," she said, "and become a thing forgotten, unremembered."

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Then the pangs of childbirth brought her to the base of the palm-tree; she said, “Oh, if only had I died before this and had become forgotten, unremembered.”

23.author: Arberry
23.text: And the birthpangs surprised her by the trunk of the palm-tree. She said, 'Would I had died ere this, and become a thing forgotten!'

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Then the birth-pangs drave her to the trunk of a palm- tree; she said: would that had died afore this and become forgotten, lost in oblivion!

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a date-palm. She said: "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight!"

23.author: Itani
23.text: The labor-pains came upon her, by the trunk of a palm-tree. She said, “I wish I had died before this, and been completely forgotten.”

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Then the birth pangs drove her to the trunk of a palm-tree and she said: "Oh, would that I had died before this and had been all forgotten."

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a date palm. She said: "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight!"

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: And the pangs of childbirth drove her unto the trunk of the palm-tree. She said: Oh, would that I had died ere this and had become a thing of naught, forgotten!

23.author: Qarai
23.text: The birth pangs brought her to the trunk of a date palm. She said, ‘I wish I had died before this and become a forgotten thing, beyond recall.’

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: And when the birthpangs came upon her by the trunk of a palmtree, she said: 'Oh, would that I had died before this and become a thing forgotten'

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten."

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: When she started to experience (the pain of) of childbirth labor, by the trunk of a palm tree in sadness she said, "Would that I had died long before and passed into oblivion."

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And the throes (of childbirth) compelled her to betake herself to the trunk of a palm tree. She said: Oh, would that I had died before this, and had been a thing quite forgotten!

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: The pains of labour drove her to the trunk of a date-palm. She said, "Oh, if only I had died before this and passed into oblivion!"

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm-tree: She cried (in her anguish): "Ah! would that I had died before this! would that I had been a thing forgotten and out of sight!"

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Faajaaha almakhadu ila jithAAi alnnakhlati qalat ya laytanee mittu qabla hatha wakuntu nasyan mansiyyan

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Then (a voice) called to her from below: "Grieve not; your Lord has made a rivulet gush forth right below you.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: (The angel) Therefore called her from below her, “Do not grieve – your Lord has made a river flow below you.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: But the one that was below her called to her, 'Nay, do not sorrow; see, thy Lord has set below thee a rivulet.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Then one cried unto her from underneath her, that grieve not, thy Lord hath placed underneath thou a rivulet.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Then [the babe 'Iesa (Jesus) or Jibrael (Gabriel)] cried unto her from below her, saying: "Grieve not! Your Lord has provided a water stream under you;

24.author: Itani
24.text: Whereupon he called her from beneath her: “Do not worry; your Lord has placed a stream beneath you.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Thereupon the angel below her cried out: "Grieve not, for your Lord has caused a stream of water to flow beneath you.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Then he cried unto her from below her, saying: "Grieve not: your Lord has provided a Sariy under you."

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Then (one) cried unto her from below her, saying: Grieve not! Thy Lord hath placed a rivulet beneath thee,

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Thereupon he called her from below her [saying,] ‘Do not grieve! Your Lord has made a spring to flow at your feet.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: He called from below to her: 'Do not sorrow, look, your Lord has provided a rivulet below you,

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Then she heard the baby saying, "Do not be sad. Your Lord has caused a stream to run at your feet.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Then (the child) called out to her from beneath her: Grieve not, surely your Lord has made a stream to flow beneath you;

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: But a voice called out to her from below, "Do not despair. Your Lord has provided a brook that runs at your feet,

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: But (a voice) cried to her from beneath the (palm-tree): "Grieve not! for thy Lord hath provided a rivulet beneath thee;

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Fanadaha min tahtiha alla tahzanee qad jaAAala rabbuki tahtaki sariyyan

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Shake the trunk of the date-palm tree, and it will drop ripe dates for you.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: “And shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards you – ripe fresh dates will fall upon you.” (This was a miracle – the date palm was dry and it was winter season.)

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Shake also to thee the palm-trunk, and there shall come tumbling upon thee dates fresh and ripe.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And shake toward thee the trunk of the palm-tree it shall drop on thee dates fresh and ripe.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: "And shake the trunk of date-palm towards you, it will let fall fresh ripe-dates upon you."

25.author: Itani
25.text: And shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards you, and it will drop ripe dates by you.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards yourself and fresh and ripe dates shall fall upon you.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: "And shake the trunk of date palm towards you, it will let fall fresh ripe dates upon you."

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And shake the trunk of the palm-tree toward thee, thou wilt cause ripe dates to fall upon thee.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: Shake the trunk of the palm tree, freshly picked dates will drop upon you.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: and shake the trunk of this palmtree it will drop fresh ripe dates upon you.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: If you shake the trunk of the palm tree, it will provide you with fresh ripe dates.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And shake towards you the trunk of the palmtree, it will drop on you fresh ripe dates:

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: and if you shake the trunk of this palm-tree, it will drop fresh ripe dates on you.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: "And shake towards thyself the trunk of the palm-tree: It will let fall fresh ripe dates upon thee.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wahuzzee ilayki bijithAAi alnnakhlati tusaqit AAalayki rutaban janiyyan

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Eat and drink, and be at peace. If you see any man, tell him: 'I have verily vowed a fast to Ar-Rahman and cannot speak to any one this day.'

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: “Therefore eat and drink and appease your eyes; so if you meet any person then say, ‘I have pledged a fast (of silence) to the Most Gracious – I will therefore not speak to any person today.’”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: Eat therefore, and drink, and be comforted; and if thou shouldst see any mortal, say, "I have vowed to the All-merciful a fast, and today I will not speak to any man.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: So eat and drink thou, and cool thine eyes; and if thou beholdest the human beings anyone, say: verily I have vowed to the Compassionate a fast, so I shall not speak today to any human being.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: "So eat and drink and be glad, and if you see any human being, say: 'Verily! I have vowed a fast unto the Most Beneficent (Allah) so I shall not speak to any human being this day. 

26.author: Itani
26.text: “So eat, and drink, and be consoled. And if you see any human, say, ‘I have vowed a fast to the Most Gracious, so I will not speak to any human today.'“

26.author: Maududi
26.text: So eat and drink and cool your eyes; and if you see any person say to him: 'Verily I have vowed a fast to the Most Compassionate Lord, and so I shall not speak to anyone today.' "

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: "So eat and drink and rejoice. And if you see any human being, say: `Verily, I have vowed a fast for the Most Gracious so I shall not speak to any human being today.,"

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: So eat and drink and be consoled. And if thou meetest any mortal, say: Lo! I have vowed a fast unto the Beneficent, and may not speak this day to any mortal.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Eat, drink, and be comforted. Then if you see any human, say, ‘‘Indeed I have vowed a fast to the All-beneficent, so I will not speak to any human today.’’ ’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Therefore eat and drink and rejoice with your eyes. If you meet any human say to him: "I have vowed a fast to the Merciful and will not speak with anyone today.'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Most Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to [any] man.' "

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Eat, drink, and rejoice. Should you see a person going by, tell him that on this day you have promised the Beneficent God to fast and never talk to any human being."

26.author: Shakir
26.text: So eat and drink and refresh the eye. Then if you see any mortal, say: Surely I have vowed a fast to the Beneficent Allah, so I shall not speak to any man today.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Eat and drink and rejoice. And if you see any human being say, 'I have vowed a fast [of silence] to the Gracious God, and will not speak with any human being today.'"

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: "So eat and drink and cool (thine) eye. And if thou dost see any man, say, 'I have vowed a fast to (Allah) Most Gracious, and this day will I enter into not talk with any human being'"

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Fakulee waishrabee waqarree AAaynan faimma tarayinna mina albashari ahadan faqoolee innee nathartu lilrrahmani sawman falan okallima alyawma insiyyan

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Then she brought the child to her people. They exclaimed: "O Mary, you have done a most astonishing thing!

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: So carrying him in her arms, she brought him to her people; they said, “O Maryam, you have indeed committed a great evil!”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Then she brought the child to her folk carrying him; and they said, 'Mary, thou hast surely committed a monstrous thing!

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Then she brought him to her nation carrying him. They said: Maryam! assuredly thou hast brought a thing unheard Of.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Then she brought him (the baby) to her people, carrying him. They said: "O Mary! Indeed you have brought a thing Fariya (an unheard mighty thing).

27.author: Itani
27.text: Then she came to her people, carrying him. They said, “O Mary, you have done something terrible.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Then she came to her people, carrying her baby. They said: "O Mary! You have committed a monstrous thing.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said: "O Mary! Indeed you have brought a thing Fariy."

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Then she brought him to her own folk, carrying him. They said: O Mary! Thou hast come with an amazing thing.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Then carrying him she brought him to her people. They said, ‘O Mary, you have certainly come up with an odd thing!

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: She came to her nation, carrying him; and they said: 'O Mary, you have committed a monstrous thing.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: She took him to her people and they said, "Mary, this is indeed an strange thing.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And she came to her people with him, carrying him (with her). They said: O Marium! surely you have done a strange thing.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Carrying her child, she brought him to her people. They said, "O Mary, you have indeed done something terrible!

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: At length she brought the (babe) to her people, carrying him (in her arms). They said: "O Mary! truly an amazing thing hast thou brought!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Faatat bihi qawmaha tahmiluhu qaloo ya maryamu laqad jiti shayan fariyyan

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a wicked person, nor your mother sinful!"

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: “O sister of Haroon, neither was your father an evil man nor was your mother of poor conduct!”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Sister of Aaron, thy father was not a wicked man, nor was thy mother a woman unchaste.'

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: O sister of Harun: thy father was not a man of evil, nor was thy mother a harlot.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: "O sister (i.e. the like) of Harun (Aaron) [not the brother of Musa (Moses), but he was another pious man at the time of Maryam (Mary)]! Your father was not a man who used to commit adultery, nor your mother was an unchaste woman."

28.author: Itani
28.text: O sister of Aaron, your father was not an evil man, and your mother was not a whore.”

28.author: Maududi
28.text: O sister of Aaron! Your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother an unchaste woman."

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: "O sister of Harun! Your father was not a man who used to commit adultery, nor your mother was an unchaste woman."

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: O sister of Aaron! Thy father was not a wicked man nor was thy mother a harlot.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: O sister of Aaron[’s lineage]! Your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother unchaste.’

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: O sister of Aaron, your father was never an evil man, nor was your mother unchaste'

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste."

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Aaron's sister, your father was not a bad man nor was your mother unchaste".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: O sister of Haroun! your father was not a bad man, nor, was your mother an unchaste woman.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Sister of Aaron, your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother an unchaste woman!"

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: "O sister of Aaron! Thy father was not a man of evil, nor thy mother a woman unchaste!"

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Ya okhta haroona ma kana abooki imraa sawin wama kanat ommuki baghiyyan

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: But she pointed towards him. "How can we talk to one," they said, "who is only an infant in the cradle?"

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: Thereupon she pointed towards the child; they said, “How can we speak to an infant who is in the cradle?”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Mary pointed to the child then; but they said, 'How shall we speak to one who is still in the cradle, a little child?'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Then she Pointed to him. They said: how shall we speak to one who is in the cradle, a child?

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Then she pointed to him. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle?"

29.author: Itani
29.text: So she pointed to him. They said, “How can we speak to an infant in the crib?”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Thereupon Mary pointed to the child. They exclaimed: "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle, a mere child?"

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Then she pointed to him. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle"

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: Then she pointed to him. They said: How can we talk to one who is in the cradle, a young boy?

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Thereat she pointed to him. They said, ‘How can we speak to one who is yet a baby in the cradle?’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: So she pointed to him (Prophet Jesus). But they replied: 'How can we speak with a baby in the cradle'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: So she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child?"

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: She pointed to the baby (and referred them to him for their answer). They said, "How can we talk to a baby in the cradle?"

29.author: Shakir
29.text: But she pointed to him. They said: How should we speak to one who was a child in the cradle?

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: She pointed to the child. They said, "How shall we talk to someone who is a child in the cradle?"

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: But she pointed to the babe. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle?"

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Faasharat ilayhi qaloo kayfa nukallimu man kana fee almahdi sabiyyan

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: "I am a servant of God," he answered. "He has given me a Book and made me a prophet,

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: The child proclaimed, “I am Allah’s bondman; He has given me the Book and made me a Herald of the Hidden (a Prophet).”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: He said, 'Lo, I am God's servant; God has given me the Book, and made me a Prophet.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: He Said: verily I am a bondman of Allah; He hath vouchsafed me the Book and made me a prophet.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: "He ['Iesa (Jesus)] said: Verily! I am a slave of Allah, He has given me the Scripture and made me a Prophet;"

30.author: Itani
30.text: He said, “I am the servant of God. He has given me the Scripture, and made me a prophet.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: The child cried out: "Verily I am Allah's servant. He has granted me the Book and has made me a Prophet

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: He said: "Verily, I am a servant of Allah, He has given me the Scripture and made me a Prophet;"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: He spake: Lo! I am the slave of Allah. He hath given me the Scripture and hath appointed me a Prophet,

30.author: Qarai
30.text: He said, ‘Indeed I am a servant of Allah! He has given me the Book and made me a prophet.

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: He (the baby) said: 'I am the worshiper of Allah. Allah has given me the Book and made me a Prophet.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: [Jesus] said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: He said, "I am the servant of God. He has given me the Book and has appointed me to be a Prophet.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: He said: Surely I am a servant of Allah; He has given me the Book and made me a prophet;

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: [But] he said, "I am God's servant. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet;

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: He said: "I am indeed a servant of Allah: He hath given me revelation and made me a prophet;

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Qala innee AAabdu Allahi ataniya alkitaba wajaAAalanee nabiyyan

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: And blessed me wherever I may be, and enjoined on me worship and zakat for as long as I live,

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: “And He has made me blessed wherever I be; and ordained upon me prayer and charity, as long as I live.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: Blessed He has made me, wherever I may be; and He has enjoined me to pray, and to give the alms, so long as I live,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And He hath made me blest wheresoever I may be, and enjoined on me the prayer and the poor-rate as long as I am alive.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: "And He has made me blessed wheresoever I be, and has enjoined on me Salat (prayer), and Zakat, as long as I live."

31.author: Itani
31.text: And has made me blessed wherever I may be; and has enjoined on me prayer and charity, so long as I live.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: and has blessed me wherever I might be and has enjoined upon me Prayer and Zakah (purifying alms) as long as I live;

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: "And He has made me blessed wherever I be, and has enjoined on me [Awsani] Salah and Zakah, as long as I live."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And hath made me blessed wheresoever I may be, and hath enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving so long as I remain alive,

31.author: Qarai
31.text: He has made me blessed, wherever I may be, and He has enjoined me to [maintain] the prayer and to [pay] the zakat as long as I live,

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: He made me to be blessed wherever I am, and He has charged me with prayer and charity for as long as I shall live.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as I remain alive

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: He has blessed me no matter where I dwell, commanded me to worship Him and pay the religious tax for as long as I live.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and He has enjoined on me prayer and poor-rate so long as I live;

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and has enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving throughout my life.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: "And He hath made me blessed wheresoever I be, and hath enjoined on me Prayer and Charity as long as I live;

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: WajaAAalanee mubarakan ayna ma kuntu waawsanee bialssalati waalzzakati ma dumtu hayyan

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And be dutiful to my mother. He has not made me haughty or rebellious.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: “And has made me good to my mother and not made me forceful, ill-fated.”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: and likewise to cherish my mother; He has not made me arrogant, unprosperous.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And dutecus Unto my mother, and hath not made me high- handed, unblest.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: "And dutiful to my mother, and made me not arrogant, unblest.

32.author: Itani
32.text: And kind to my mother, and He did not make me a disobedient rebel.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: and has made me dutiful to my mother. He has not made me oppressive, nor bereft of God's blessings.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: "And to be dutiful to my mother, and made me not arrogant, unblessed."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And (hath made me) dutiful toward her who bore me, and hath not made me arrogant, unblest.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: and to be good to my mother, and He has not made me self-willed and wretched.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: (He has made me) kind to my mother; He has not made me arrogant, unprosperous.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And [made me] dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: He has commanded me to be good to my parents and has not made me an arrogant rebellious person.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me insolent, unblessed;

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: He has made me dutiful toward my mother, and He has not made me arrogant or wicked.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: "(He) hath made me kind to my mother, and not overbearing or miserable;

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Wabarran biwalidatee walam yajAAalnee jabbaran shaqiyyan

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: There was peace on me the day I was born, and will be the day I die, and on the day I will be raised from the dead."

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: “And peace is upon me the day I was born, and on the day I shall taste death, and on the day I will be raised alive.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Peace be upon me, the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised up alive!'

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And peace be on me the day was born and the day die and the day I shall be raised up alive.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: "And Salam (peace) be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive!"

33.author: Itani
33.text: So Peace is upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the Day I get resurrected alive.”

33.author: Maududi
33.text: Peace be upon me the day I was born and the day I will die, and the day I will be raised up alive."

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: "And Salam (peace) be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive!"

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: Peace on me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive!

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Peace to me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised alive.’

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Peace be upon me on the day I was born, and on the day I die; and on the day when I shall be raised up alive'

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive."

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: I was born with peace and I shall die and be brought to life again with peace."

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And peace on me on the day I was born, and on the day I die, and on the day I am raised to life.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: Blessed was I on the day I was born, and blessed I shall be on the day I die and on the day I am raised to life again."

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "So peace is on me the day I was born, the day that I die, and the day that I shall be raised up to life (again)"!

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Waalssalamu AAalayya yawma wulidtu wayawma amootu wayawma obAAathu hayyan

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: This was Jesus, son of Mary: A true account they contend about.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: This is Eisa (Jesus), the son of Maryam; a true statement, in which they doubt.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: That is Jesus, son of Mary, in word of truth, concerning which they are doubting.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Such is 'lsa, son of Maryam: this Is the word of truth wherein they are doubting.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Such is 'Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary). (it is) a statement of truth, about which they doubt (or dispute).

34.author: Itani
34.text: That is Jesus son of Mary—the Word of truth about which they doubt.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: This is Jesus, the son of Mary; and this is the truth about him concerning which they are in doubt.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: Such is `Isa, son of Maryam. Qawlal-Haqq [it is a statement of truth] about which they doubt (or dispute).

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Such was Jesus, son of Mary: (this is) a statement of the truth concerning which they doubt.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: That is Jesus, son of Mary, a Word of the Real concerning whom they are in doubt.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Such was (Prophet) Jesus, the son of Mary. A saying of truth, concerning what they doubt.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: Such was the true story of Jesus, the son of Mary, about which they dispute bitterly.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Such is Isa, son of Marium; (this is) the saying of truth about which they dispute.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Such was Jesus, the son of Mary. That is the whole truth, about which they still dispute:

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Such (was) Jesus the son of Mary: (it is) a statement of truth, about which they (vainly) dispute.

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Thalika AAeesa ibnu maryama qawla alhaqqi allathee feehi yamtaroona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: It does not behove God to have a son. Too immaculate is He! When He decrees a thing He has only to say: "Be", and it is.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: It does not befit Allah to appoint someone as His son – Purity is to Him! When He ordains a matter, He just commands it, “Be” – and it thereupon happens.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: It is not for God to take a son unto Him. Glory be to Him! When He decrees a thing, He but says to it 'Be,' and it is.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Allah is not one to take to Himself a son. Hallowed be He! whensoever He decreeth an affair he only Saith to it: be, and it becometh.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: It befits not (the Majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son [this refers to the slander of Christians against Allah, by saying that 'Iesa (Jesus) is the son of Allah]. Glorified (and Exalted be He above all that they associate with Him). When He decrees a thing, He only says to it, "Be!" and it is.

35.author: Itani
35.text: It is not for God to have a child—glory be to Him. To have anything done, He says to it, “Be,” and it becomes.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: It does not befit Allah to take for Himself a son. Glory be to Him! When He decrees a thing He only says: "Be" and it is.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: It befits not (the majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son. Glorified be He. When He decrees a thing, He only says to it: "Be!" -- and it is.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: It befitteth not (the Majesty of) Allah that He should take unto Himself a son. Glory be to Him! When He decreeth a thing, He saith unto it only: Be! and it is.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: It is not for Allah to take a son. Immaculate is He! When He decides on a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: It is not for Allah to take a son! Exaltations to Him! When He decrees a thing He only says: 'Be' and it is.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: It is not [befitting] for Allah to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: God is too Exalted to have a son. When He decides to bring some thing into existence He needs only command it to exist and it comes into existence.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: It beseems not Allah that He should take to Himself a son, glory to be Him; when He has decreed a matter He only says to it "Be," and it is.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
