35.text: it does not befit the majesty of God that He should beget a son. Glory be to Him! He is far above that: when He decrees something, He says only, "Be!" and it is.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: It is not befitting to (the majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son. Glory be to Him! when He determines a matter, He only says to it, "Be", and it is.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Ma kana lillahi an yattakhitha min waladin subhanahu itha qada amran fainnama yaqoolu lahu kun fayakoonu

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: (Jesus only said:) "Surely God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is the straight path."

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And said Eisa, “Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord – therefore worship Him; this is the Straight Path.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: Surely God is my Lord, and your Lord; So serve you Him. This is a straight path.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And verily Allah is my Lord and your Lord; so worship Him; this is a way straight.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: ['Iesa (Jesus) said]: "And verily Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him (Alone). That is the Straight Path. (Allah's Religion of Islamic Monotheism which He did ordain for all of His Prophets)." [Tafsir At-Tabari]

36.author: Itani
36.text: “God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path.”

36.author: Maududi
36.text: (Jesus had said): "Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him alone. This is the Straight Way."

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: [`Isa said]: "And verily, Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. That is the straight path."

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And lo! Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So serve Him. That is the right path.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: [And Jesus said,] ‘Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. This is a straight path.’

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord therefore worship Him. That is the Straight Path.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: [Jesus said], "And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path."

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: (Baby Jesus said), "Worship God who is my Lord as well as yours. This is the straight path".

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, therefore serve Him; this is the right path.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him alone. That is the right path.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Verily Allah is my Lord and your Lord: Him therefore serve ye: this is a Way that is straight.

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Wainna Allaha rabbee warabbukum faoAAbudoohu hatha siratun mustaqeemun

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Yet the sectarians differed among themselves. Alas for the unbelievers when they see the Terrible Day!

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Then groups among them differed; so ruin is for the disbelievers from the witnessing of a Great Day.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: But the parties have fallen into variance among themselves; then woe to those who disbelieve for the scene of a dreadful day.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Then the sects have differed among themselves; so woe to those who disbelieve in the witness of a Day Mighty!

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Then the sects differed [i.e. the Christians about 'Iesa (Jesus)], so woe unto the disbelievers [those who gave false witness by saying that 'Iesa (Jesus) is the son of Allah] from the meeting of a great Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection, when they will be thrown in the blazing Fire).

37.author: Itani
37.text: But the various factions differed among themselves. So woe to those who disbelieve from the scene of a tremendous Day.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: But different parties began to dispute with one another. A dreadful woe awaits on that great Day for those that reject the Truth.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Then the sects differed, so woe unto the disbelievers from the meeting of a great Day.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: The sects among them differ: but woe unto the disbelievers from the meeting of an awful Day.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to the faithless at the scene of a tremendous day.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: But the parties have fallen into variance among themselves, then woe to those who disbelieve for the scene of a Dreadful Day.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Then the factions differed [concerning Jesus] from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: (The followers of Jesus) turned themselves into quarrelling sects. The disbelievers shall face a woeful condition on the great Day (of Judgment).

37.author: Shakir
37.text: But parties from among them disagreed with each other, so woe to those who disbelieve, because of presence on a great day.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Yet different groups differed among themselves. How awful it will be for those who have rejected the truth when a dreadful Day arrives!

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: But the sects differ among themselves: and woe to the unbelievers because of the (coming) Judgment of a Momentous Day!

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Faikhtalafa alahzabu min baynihim fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo min mashhadi yawmin AAatheemin

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: How keenly would they hear and see when they come before Us then, even though today the evil-doers are lost in palpable error.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Much will they listen and much will they see, on the Day when they come to Us, but today the unjust are in open error.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: How well they will hear and see on the day they come to Us! But the evildoers even today are in error manifest.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: How wondrous in their hearing and their sight will they be the Day they come unto Us! But to-day the wrong doers are in error manifest.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: How clearly will they (polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah) see and hear, the Day when they will appear before Us! But the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers) today are in plain error.

38.author: Itani
38.text: Listen to them and watch for them the Day they come to Us. But the wrongdoers today are completely lost.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: How well shall they hear and how well shall they see on the Day they come to Us! But today the evil-doers are in manifest error.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: How clearly will they see and hear, the Day when they will appear before Us! But the wrongdoers today are in plain error.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: See and hear them on the Day they come unto Us! yet the evil-doers are to-day in error manifest.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: How well they will hear and how well they will see on the day when they come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are in manifest error.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: How well they will hear and see on the Day when they come before Us! The evildoers are today in clear error.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: How [clearly] they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: (Muhammad), how clearly they will hear and see on the day when they will be brought into Our presence. Today the wrong doers are in manifest error.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: How clearly shall they hear and how clearly shall they see on the day when they come to Us; but the unjust this day are in manifest error.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: How sharp of hearing, how sharp of sight they will be when they come to Us. But today, these evil-doers are obviously lost in error.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: How plainly will they see and hear, the Day that they will appear before Us! but the unjust today are in error manifest!

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: AsmiAA bihim waabsir yawma yatoonana lakini alththalimoona alyawma fee dalalin mubeenin

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: Warn them of that day of pining when all matters will have been settled, though they would still be unaware and unbelieving (of the truth).

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And warn them of the Day of Regret when the matter will have been decided; and they are in neglect, and they do not accept faith.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: Warn thou them of the day of anguish, when the matter shall be determined, and they yet heedless and unbelieving.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And warn thou them of thy Day of Sighing when the affair shall have been decreed while yet they are in negligence and are not believing.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And warn them (O Muhammad SAW) of the Day of grief and regrets, when the case has been decided, while (now) they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not.

39.author: Itani
39.text: And warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded. Yet they are heedless, and they do not believe.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: (O Muhammad), warn those who are steeped in heedlessness and are obstinately rejecting the truth that the Day shall come when things will be finally decided and they shall be left with utter remorse.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And warn them of the Day of grief and regrets, when the case has been decided, while (now) they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: And warn them of the Day of anguish when the case hath been decided. Now they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: Warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be decided, while they are [yet] heedless and do not have faith.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: Warn them of that Day of Anguish, when the matter is determined whilst heedlessly they disbelieve.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and [yet], they are in [a state of] heedlessness, and they do not believe.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: Warn them of the woeful day when the final decree will be issued; they are neglectful and faithless.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And warn them of the day of intense regret, when the matter shall have been decided; and they are (now) in negligence and they do not believe.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: Warn them of [the coming of] the Day of Remorse, when everything will have been decided, while they are heedless and do not believe.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: But warn them of the Day of Distress, when the matter will be determined: for (behold,) they are negligent and they do not believe!

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Waanthirhum yawma alhasrati ith qudiya alamru wahum fee ghaflatin wahum la yuminoona

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Verily We shall inherit the earth and whosoever is on it, and to Us they will return.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: Indeed We shall inherit the earth and all that is on it, and only towards Us will they return.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Surely We shall inherit the earth and all that are upon it, and unto Us they shall be returned.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Verily We! We shall inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon: and Unto Us they shall be returned.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Verily! We will inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon. And to Us they all shall be returned,

40.author: Itani
40.text: It is We who will inherit the earth and everyone on it, and to Us they will be returned.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Ultimately, We shall inherit the earth and whatever is on it; to Us shall they be returned.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Verily, We will inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon. And to Us they all shall be returned.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Lo! We, only We, inherit the earth and all who are thereon, and unto Us they are returned.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Indeed We shall inherit the earth and whoever there is on it, and to Us they shall be brought back.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: For We shall inherit the earth and all that are on it. To Us, they shall return.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: We are the heirs of the earth and those living in it will all return to Us.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Surely We inherit the earth and all those who are on it, and to Us they shall be returned.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: It is We who will inherit the earth and all who dwell upon it: they shall all return to Us.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: It is We Who will inherit the earth, and all beings thereon: to Us will they all be returned.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Inna nahnu narithu alarda waman AAalayha wailayna yurjaAAoona

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Commemorate Abraham in the Book: He was upright, a prophet.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And remember Ibrahim in the Book; he was very truthful, a Herald of the Hidden (a Prophet).

41.author: Arberry
41.text: And mention in the Book Abraham; surely he was a true man, a Prophet.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And mention thou in the Book Ibrahim; verily he was a man of truth, a prophet.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Ibrahim (Abraham). Verily! He was a man of truth, a Prophet.

41.author: Itani
41.text: And mention in the Scripture Abraham. He was a man of truth, a prophet.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: (O Muhammad), recite in the Book the account of Abraham. Most surely he was a man of truth, a Prophet.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And mention in the Book, Ibrahim. Verily, he was a man of truth, a Prophet.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And make mention (O Muhammad) in the Scripture of Abraham. Lo! he was a saint, a prophet.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: And mention in the Book Abraham. Indeed, he was a truthful man and a prophet.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Mention in the Book Abraham; He was truthful and a Prophet.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: Mention the story of Abraham, the truthful Prophet, in the Book (the Quran)

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And mention Ibrahim in the Book; surely he was a truthful man, a prophet.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Also recount the story of Abraham in the Book. He was a man of truth, and a prophet.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: (Also mention in the Book (the story of) Abraham: He was a man of Truth, a prophet.

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Waothkur fee alkitabi ibraheema innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyyan

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Remember, when he said to his father: "O my father, why do you worship that which can neither hear nor see nor even profit you the least?

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: When he said to his father,* “O my father – why do you worship one which neither hears nor sees, and cannot benefit you in any way?” (* His uncle Azar.)

42.author: Arberry
42.text: When he said to his father, 'Father, why worshippest thou that which neither hears nor sees, nor avails thee anything?

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Recall what time he said unto his father:, O my father! wherefore worshippest thou that which heareth not and seeth not, nor availeth thee at all?

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: When he said to his father: "O my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and cannot avail you in anything?

42.author: Itani
42.text: He said to his father, “O my father, why do you worship what can neither hear, nor see, nor benefit you in any way?

42.author: Maududi
42.text: (And remind people) when he said to his father: "Father! Why do you worship that which neither sees nor hears, and which can be of no avail to you?

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: When he said to his father: "O my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and cannot avail you in anything"

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: When he said unto his father: O my father! Why worshippest thou that which heareth not nor seeth, nor can in aught avail thee?

42.author: Qarai
42.text: When he said to his father, ‘Father! Why do you worship that which neither hears nor sees, and is of no avail to you in any way?

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: He said to his father: 'O father, why worship that which can neither see nor hear, nor helps you in anything?

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: [Mention] when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all?

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: who asked his father, "Father, why do you worship something that can neither hear nor see nor help you at all?

42.author: Shakir
42.text: When he said to his father; O my father! why do you worship what neither hears nor sees, nor does it avail you in the least:

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: He said to his father, "Why do you worship something that can neither hear nor see nor benefit you in any way?

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Behold, he said to his father: "O my father! why worship that which heareth not and seeth not, and can profit thee nothing?

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Ith qala liabeehi ya abati lima taAAbudu ma la yasmaAAu wala yubsiru wala yughnee AAanka shayan

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: O my father, to me has come such knowledge as never came to you. So follow me that I may show you the right path.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: “O my father, indeed a knowledge has come to me which did not come to you – therefore follow me, I will show you the Straight Path.”

43.author: Arberry
43.text: Father, there has come to me knowledge such as came not to thee; so follow me, and I will guide thee on a level path.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: O My father! verily there hath come to me of the knowledge which hath not come unto thee; so follow me, and I shall guide thee to a path even.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: "O my father! Verily! There has come to me of knowledge that which came not unto you. So follow me. I will guide you to a Straight Path.

43.author: Itani
43.text: O my father, there has come to me knowledge that never came to you. So follow me, and I will guide you along a straight way.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Father, a knowledge that has not reached you has come to me. So follow me that I may guide you to a Straight Way.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: "O my father! Verily, there has come to me the knowledge of that which came not unto you. So follow me, I will guide you to the straight path."

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: O my father! Lo! there hath come unto me of knowledge that which came not unto thee. So follow me, and I will lead thee on a right path.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Father! Indeed a knowledge has already come to me, which has not come to you. So follow me that I may guide you to a right path.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Father, knowledge has come to me which has not come you, therefore, follow me. I will guide you to a Level Path.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: O my father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Father, I have received the knowledge which has not been given to you. Follow me; I shall guide you to the right path.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: O my father! truly the knowledge has come to me which has not come to you, therefore follow me, I will guide you on a right path:

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Father, I have been given some knowledge which has not come to you, so follow me: I shall guide you along a straight path.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: "O my father! to me hath come knowledge which hath not reached thee: so follow me: I will guide thee to a way that is even and straight.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Ya abati innee qad jaanee mina alAAilmi ma lam yatika faittabiAAnee ahdika siratan sawiyyan

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Why do you worship Satan, O father? Verily Satan was disobedient to Ar-Rahman.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: “O my father, do not be a bondman of the devil; indeed the devil is disobedient towards the Most Gracious.”

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Father, serve not Satan; surely Satan is a rebel against the All-merciful.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: O My father! worship not the satan; hath been Unto the Compassionate a rebel.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: "O my father! Worship not Shaitan (Satan). Verily! Shaitan (Satan) has been a rebel against the Most Beneficent (Allah).

44.author: Itani
44.text: O my father, do not worship the devil. The devil is disobedient to the Most Gracious.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Father, do not serve Satan, for Satan has indeed been a persistent rebel against the Most Compassionate Lord.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: "O my father! Worship not Shaytan. Verily, Shaytan has been a rebel against the Most Gracious."

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: O my father! Serve not the devil. Lo! the devil is a rebel unto the Beneficent.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Father! Do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan is disobedient to the All-beneficent.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Father, do not worship satan; for satan has rebelled against the Merciful.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: O my father, do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Most Merciful, disobedient.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Father, do not worship satan; he has disobeyed the Beneficent God.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: O my father! serve not the Shaitan, surely the Shaitan is disobedient to the Beneficent Allah:

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Father! Do not worship Satan -- for, truly, Satan is a rebel against the Most Gracious One!

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: "O my father! serve not Satan: for Satan is a rebel against (Allah) Most Gracious.

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Ya abati la taAAbudi alshshaytana inna alshshaytana kana lilrrahmani AAasiyyan

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: O my father, I fear lest a punishment from Ar-Rahman should befall you, and you should become a friend of the Devil."

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: “O my father, I fear that a punishment from the Most Gracious may reach you, so you would become a companion of the devil.”

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Father, I fear that some chastisement from the All-merciful will smite thee, so that thou becomest a friend to Satan.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: O My father! verily I fear that there may touch thee a torment from the Compassionate so that thou become to the Satan a companin.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: "O my father! Verily! I fear lest a torment from the Most Beneficent (Allah) overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shaitan (Satan) (in the Hell-fire)." [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi]

45.author: Itani
45.text: O my father, I fear that a punishment from the Most Gracious will afflict you, and you become an ally of the devil.”

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Father, I fear that a punishment from the Most Compassionate Lord might strike you and you may end up as one of Satan's companions?"

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: "O my father! Verily, I fear lest a torment from the Most Gracious should overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shaytan."

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: O my father! Lo! I fear lest a punishment from the Beneficent overtake thee so that thou become a comrade of the devil.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Father! I am indeed afraid that a punishment from the All-beneficent will befall you, and you will become Satan’s accomplice.’

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Father, I fear that the punishment of the Merciful will fall upon you and you will become a guide of satan'

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Most Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion [in Hellfire]."

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: Father, I am afraid that the Beneficent God's torment will strike you and you will become a friend of satan."

45.author: Shakir
45.text: O my father! surely I fear that a punishment from the Beneficent Allah should afflict you so that you should be a friend of the Shaitan.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Father, indeed I fear lest a punishment from the Gracious One afflict you, and you become a friend of Satan."

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: "O my father! I fear lest a Penalty afflict thee from (Allah) Most Gracious, so that thou become to Satan a friend."

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Ya abati innee akhafu an yamassaka AAathabun mina alrrahmani fatakoona lilshshyatani waliyyan

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: He said: "Are you averse to my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I shall have you stoned to death. So go away for a while from me."

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: He said, “What! You turn away from my Gods, O Ibrahim? If you do not desist, I will certainly stone you, and keep no relation with me for a long while.”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: Said he, 'What, art thou shrinking from my gods, Abraham? Surely, if thou givest not over, I shall stone thee; so forsake me now for some while.'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: He said: art thou averse to my gods, O Ibrahim? If thou desistest not, surely I shall stone thee; and depart from me for ever so long.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: He (the father) said: "Do you reject my gods, O Ibrahim (Abraham)? If you stop not (this), I will indeed stone you. So get away from me safely before I punish you."

46.author: Itani
46.text: He said, “Are you renouncing my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will stone you. So leave me alone for a while.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: The father said: "Abraham, have you turned away from my gods? If you do not give this up, I shall stone you to death. Now begone from me forever."

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: He (the father) said: "Do you revile my gods, O Ibrahim If you stop not (this), I will La'arjumannak. So get away from me Maliyan."

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: He said: Rejectest thou my gods, O Abraham? If thou cease not, I shall surely stone thee. Depart from me a long while!

46.author: Qarai
46.text: He said, ‘Abraham! Are you renouncing my gods? If you do not desist, I will stone you. Get away from me for a long while.’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: But he replied: 'Do you shrink away from my gods, Abraham? Surely, if you do not cease I will stone you, so leave me for awhile'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: [His father] said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time."

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: His father replied, "Abraham, are you telling me to give-up my gods? If you will not stop this, I shall stone you to death. Leave my house and do not come back again".

46.author: Shakir
46.text: He said: Do you dislike my gods, O Ibrahim? If you do not desist I will certainly revile you, and leave me for a time.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: [His father] said, "Do you reject my deities, Abraham? If you do not desist, I shall surely stone you to death. Keep out of my way!"

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: (The father) replied: "Dost thou hate my gods, O Abraham? If thou forbear not, I will indeed stone thee: Now get away from me for a good long while!"

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Qala araghibun anta AAan alihatee ya ibraheemu lain lam tantahi laarjumannaka waohjurnee maliyyan

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: He answered: "Peace be on you. I will seek forgiveness of my Lord for you. He has been gracious to me.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: He said, “Stop it – peace be upon you; I shall seek forgiveness for you from my Lord; indeed He is very kind to me.”

47.author: Arberry
47.text: He said, 'Peace be upon thee! I will ask my Lord to forgive thee; surely He is ever gracious to me.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: Ibrahim said: peace be on thee! anon shall ask forgiveness of my Lord for thee; verily He is unto me ever so soicitous.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: Ibrahim (Abraham) said: "Peace be on you! I will ask Forgiveness of my Lord for you. Verily! He is unto me, Ever Most Gracious.

47.author: Itani
47.text: He said, “Peace be upon you. I will ask my Lord to forgive you; He has been Kind to me.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: Abraham answered: "Peace be upon you. I shall seek pardon for you from my Lord. My Lord has always been kind to Me.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: Ibrahim said: "Peace be on you! I will ask forgiveness of my Lord for you. Verily, He is unto me Hafiyyan.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: He said: Peace be unto thee! I shall ask forgiveness of my Lord for thee. Lo! He was ever gracious unto me.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: He said, ‘Peace be to you! I shall plead with my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He is gracious to me.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: 'Peace be on you' he (Abraham) said, 'I shall call upon my Lord to forgive you, for to me He has been gracious.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: [Abraham] said, "Peace will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: Abraham said, "Peace be with you. I shall ask my Lord to forgive you; He has been gracious to me.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: He said: Peace be on you, I will pray to my Lord to forgive you; surely He is ever Affectionate to me:

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: Abraham replied, "Peace be on you: I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness, He has indeed been gracious to me,

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Abraham said: "Peace be on thee: I will pray to my Lord for thy forgiveness: for He is to me Most Gracious.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Qala salamun AAalayka saastaghfiru laka rabbee innahu kana bee hafiyyan

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: I will leave you and those you invoke apart from God, and pray to my Lord. Haply in praying to my Lord I will not be deprived."

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: "And I shall remain separated from you and all that you worship other than Allah and shall worship my Lord; possibly, by worshipping my Lord, I will not be amongst the unfortunate.”

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Now I will go apart from you and that you call upon, apart from God; I will call upon my Lord, and haply I shall not be, in calling upon my Lord, unprosperous.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And I renounce you and that unto which ye call beside Allah; and I shall call unto my Lord; belike in calling unto my Lord I shall not be unblest.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: "And I shall turn away from you and from those whom you invoke besides Allah. And I shall call on my Lord; and I hope that I shall not be unblest in my invocation to my Lord."

48.author: Itani
48.text: And I will withdraw from you, and from what you pray to instead of God. And I will pray to my Lord, and I hope I will not be disappointed in my prayer to my Lord.”

48.author: Maududi
48.text: I shall withdraw from you and all that you call upon beside Allah. I shall only call upon my Lord. I trust the prayer to my Lord will not go unanswered."

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And I shall turn away from you and from those whom you invoke besides Allah. And I shall call upon my Lord, and I certainly hope that I shall not be unblessed in my invocation to my Lord."

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: I shall withdraw from you and that unto which ye pray beside Allah, and I shall pray unto my Lord. It may be that, in prayer unto my Lord, I shall not be unblest.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: I dissociate myself from you and whatever you invoke besides Allah. I will supplicate my Lord. Hopefully, I will not be disappointed in supplicating my Lord.’

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: Now I will go away from you and that you call upon, other than Allah. I will call on my Lord. Perhaps I shall not be unblessed in calling my Lord'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allah and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy."

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: I shall stay away from you and what you worship instead of God. I worship my Lord and hope that my prayers will not be ignored."

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And I will withdraw from you and what you call on besides Allah, and I will call upon my Lord; may be I shall not remain unblessed in calling upon my Lord.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: I will separate myself from you and from whatever you call upon besides God, and I will pray only to my Lord. It may well be that, in calling on my Lord, I will not be disappointed."

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: "And I will turn away from you (all) and from those whom ye invoke besides Allah: I will call on my Lord: perhaps, by my prayer to my Lord, I shall be not unblest."

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: WaaAAtazilukum wama tadAAoona min dooni Allahi waadAAoo rabbee AAasa alla akoona biduAAai rabbee shaqiyyan

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Thus, when he left them and the (idols) they worshipped, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet,

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: So when he had separated from them and what they worshipped other than Allah, We bestowed him Ishaq and Yaqub; and We made each of them a Herald of the Hidden.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: So, when he went apart from them and that they were serving, apart from God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a Prophet;

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Then when he had renounced them and that which they worshipped beside Allah, We best owed on him Ishaq and Yaqub and each one We made a prophet!

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and each one of them We made a Prophet.

49.author: Itani
49.text: When he withdrew from them, and from what they worship besides God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob. And each We made a prophet.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Thereupon Abraham dissociated himself from his people and the deities they worshipped instead of Allah, and We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob and made each of them a Prophet;

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaq and Ya`qub, and each one of them We made a Prophet.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: So, when he had withdrawn from them and that which they were worshipping beside Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob. Each of them We made a prophet.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: So when he had left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a prophet.

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: So when he turned away from them and those they worshipped, other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob. Each of them We made a Prophet,

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: When (Abraham) rejected his people and what they worshipped instead of God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob and made both of them Prophets.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: So when he withdrew from them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub, and each one of them We made a prophet.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: So when he had separated himself from them and from what they worshipped besides God, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and We made each of them a prophet.

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: When he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and each one of them We made a prophet.

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Falamma iAAtazalahum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi wahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba wakullan jaAAalna nabiyyan

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: And bestowed on them some of Our blessings, and gave them high renown.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And We gave them Our mercy, and assigned for them a true and high repute.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: and We gave them of Our mercy, and We appointed unto them a tongue of truthfulness, sublime.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And We bestowed on them of Our mercy, and We made for them a renown lofty.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And We gave them of Our Mercy (a good provision in plenty), and We granted them honour on the tongues (of all the nations, i.e. everybody remembers them with a good praise).

50.author: Itani
50.text: And We gave them freely of Our mercy, and gave them a noble reputation of truth.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: and We bestowed on them Our mercy, and granted them a truly lofty renown.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And We gave them of Our mercy, and We granted them Sidqin `Aliyyan on the tongues.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And we gave them of Our mercy, and assigned to them a high and true renown.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: And We gave them out of Our mercy, and conferred on them a worthy and lofty repute.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: and We gave them of Our Mercy and We appointed to them a truthful, highly sincere tongue,

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: We granted them Our blessing and high renown.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And We granted to them of Our mercy, and We left (behind them) a truthful mention of eminence for them.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: We granted them Our mercy and bestowed on them true and high renown.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: And We bestowed of Our Mercy on them, and We granted them lofty honour on the tongue of truth.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Wawahabna lahum min rahmatina wajaAAalna lahum lisana sidqin AAaliyyan

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Commemorate Moses in the Book. He was a chosen one, both an apostle and a prophet.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And remember Moosa in the Book; he was indeed a chosen one, and he was a Noble Messenger, a Herald of the Hidden.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: And mention in the Book Moses; he was devoted, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And mention thou in the Book Musa: verily he was single- hearted, and was an apostle, prophet.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And mention in the Book (this Quran) Musa (Moses). Verily! He was chosen and he was a Messenger (and) a Prophet.

51.author: Itani
51.text: And mention in the Scripture Moses. He was dedicated. He was a messenger and a prophet.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: And recite in the Book the account of Moses. He was a chosen one, a Messenger, a Prophet.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And mention in the Book, Musa. Verily, he was Mukhlasan and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And make mention in the Scripture of Moses. Lo! he was chosen, and he was a messenger (of Allah), a prophet.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: And mention in the Book Moses. Indeed he was exclusively dedicated [to Allah], and an apostle and prophet.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Mention in the Book, Moses. He was devoted, a Messenger, and a Prophet.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: (Muhammad), mention in the Book (the Quran) the story of Moses. Moses was a sincere person, a Messengers and a Prophet.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And mention Musa in the Book; surely he was one purified, and he was an apostle, a prophet.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Tell also of Moses in the Book. He was indeed a chosen one, and was a messenger and a prophet.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Moses: for he was specially chosen, and he was a messenger (and) a prophet.

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Waothkur fee alkitabi moosa innahu kana mukhlasan wakana rasoolan nabiyyan

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: We called him from the right side of the Mount, and brought him close for communion;

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: We called him from the right side of the mountain Tur, and brought him close to reveal Our secret.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: We called to him from the right side Of the Mount, and We brought him near in communion.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: And We cried unto him from the right side of the mount, and We drew him nigh for whispering.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: And We called him from the right side of the Mount, and made him draw near to Us for a talk with him [Musa (Moses)].

52.author: Itani
52.text: And We called him from the right side of the Mount, and brought him near in communion.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: We called out to him from the right side of the Mount, and We drew him near to Us by communing to him in secret,

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: And We called him from the right side of the Mount (Tur), and made him draw near to Us for a talk with him.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: We called him from the right slope of the Mount, and brought him nigh in communion.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: We called him from the right side of the Mount and We drew him near [to Ourselves] for confidential discourse.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: We called out to him from the right side of the mountain, and drew him near in (Divine) conversation.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: And We called him from the side of the mount at [his] right and brought him near, confiding [to him].

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: We called him from the right side of Mount Sinai and drew him close for communication.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: And We called to him from the blessed side of the mountain, and We made him draw nigh, holding communion (with Us).

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: We called out to him from the right side of the mount and made him draw near to be in close communion with Us;

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: And we called him from the right side of Mount (Sinai), and made him draw near to Us, for mystic (converse).

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Wanadaynahu min janibi alttoori alaymani waqarrabnahu najiyyan

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: And bestowed on him his brother Aaron, a prophet, through Our benevolence.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And with Our mercy We bestowed upon him his brother Haroon, a Prophet.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: And We gave him his brother Aaron, of Our mercy, a Prophet.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And We bestowed on him, out of Our mercy, his brother Harun, a prophet.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And We bestowed on him his brother Harun (Aaron), (also) a Prophet, out of Our Mercy.

53.author: Itani
53.text: And We granted him, out of Our mercy, his brother Aaron, a prophet.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: and out of Our mercy We appointed his brother Aaron, a Prophet (that he may assist him).

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And We granted him his brother Harun, (also) a Prophet, out of Our mercy.

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And We bestowed upon him of Our mercy his brother Aaron, a prophet (likewise).

53.author: Qarai
53.text: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron, a prophet.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: From Our Mercy We gave him, his brother Aaron, a Prophet.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: Out of Our mercy We gave him his brother Aaron who himself was a Prophet.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And We gave to him out of Our mercy his brother Haroun a prophet.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: and We gave him as his helper, out of Our mercy, his brother Aaron, having made him a prophet.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: And, out of Our Mercy, We gave him his brother Aaron, (also) a prophet.

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Wawahabna lahu min rahmatina akhahu haroona nabiyyan

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: Commemorate Ishmael in the Book. He was true of his promise, and a messenger, a prophet.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: And remember Ismail in the Book; he was indeed true to his promise and was a Noble Messenger, a Prophet.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: And mention in the Book Ishmael; he was true to his promise, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: And mention thou in the Book lsma'i; verily he was true in promise, and was apostle, prophet.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Isma'il (Ishmael). Verily! He was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.

54.author: Itani
54.text: And mention in the Scripture Ishmael. He was true to his promise, and was a messenger, a prophet.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: And recite in the Book the account of Ishmael. He was ever true to his promise, and was a Messenger, a Prophet.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: And mention in the Book, Isma`il. Verily, he was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: And make mention in the Scripture of Ishmael. Lo! he was a keeper of his promise, and he was a messenger (of Allah), a prophet.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: And mention in the Book Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and an apostle and prophet.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: And mention in the Book, Ishmael; he too was true to his promise, a Messenger and a Prophet.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: Mention in the Book (the Quran) the story of Ishmael; he was true to his promise, a Messengers and a Prophet.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: And mention Ismail in the Book; surely he was truthful in (his) promise, and he was an apostle, a prophet.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: Tell also of Ishmael in the Book. He was true to his promise and was a messenger and a prophet.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Isma'il: He was (strictly) true to what he promised, and he was a messenger (and) a prophet.

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Waothkur fee alkitabi ismaAAeela innahu kana sadiqa alwaAAdi wakana rasoolan nabiyyan

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: He enjoined on his household worship and zakat, and he was obedient to his Lord.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: He used to command his people to offer prayer and give charity, and was liked by his Lord.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: He bade his people to pray and to give the alms, and he was pleasing to his Lord.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And he was wont to command his household to the Prayer and the poor-rate, and he was with his Lord an approved one. *Chapter: 19

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: And he used to enjoin on his family and his people As-Salat (the prayers) and the Zakat, and his Lord was pleased with him.

55.author: Itani
55.text: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and charity, and he was pleasing to his Lord.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: He enjoined his household to observe Prayer and to give Zakah (purifying alms); and his Lord was well pleased with him.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: And he would enjoin on his family and his people the Salah and the Zakah, and his Lord was pleased with him.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: He enjoined upon his people worship and almsgiving, and was acceptable in the sight of his Lord.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: He used to bid his family to [maintain] the prayer and to [pay] the zakat, and was pleasing to his Lord.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: And he ordered his people to pray and to give charity and his Lord was pleased with him.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: He would order his people to worship God and pay the religious tax. His Lord was pleased with him.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And he enjoined on his family prayer and almsgiving, and was one in whom his Lord was well pleased.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: He exhorted his people to prayer and almsgiving, and his Lord was pleased with him.

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: He used to enjoin on his people Prayer and Charity, and he was most acceptable in the sight of his Lord.

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Wakana yamuru ahlahu bialssalati waalzzakati wakana AAinda rabbihi mardiyyan

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: Commemorate Enoch in the Book. He was a truthful person and a prophet,

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: And remember Idrees in the Book; he was indeed very truthful, a Prophet.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: And mention in the Book Idris; he was a true man, a Prophet.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: And mention thou in the book ldris, verily he was a man of truth, a prophet. *Chapter: 19

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Idris (Enoch). Verily! He was a man of truth, (and) a Prophet.

56.author: Itani
56.text: And mention in the Scripture Enoch. He was a man of truth, a prophet.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: And recite in the Book the account of Idris. He was a man of truth, a Prophet;

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: And mention in the Book, Idris. Verily, he was a man of truth, (and) a Prophet.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: And make mention in the Scripture of Idris. Lo! he was a saint, a prophet;

56.author: Qarai
56.text: And mention in the Book Idrees. Indeed, he was a truthful one and a prophet,

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: And mention in the Book, Idris; he too was of the truth and a Prophet,

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: Mention in the Book (the Quran) the story of Idris (Enoch); he was a truthful Prophet.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: And mention Idris in the Book; surely he was a truthful man, a prophet,

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: Tell also of Idris in the Book. He was a man of truth and a prophet.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: Also mention in the Book the case of Idris: He was a man of truth (and sincerity), (and) a prophet:

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Waothkur fee alkitabi idreesa innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyyan

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: And We raised him to an exalted station.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: And We lifted him to a high position. (Living with soul & body in heaven, after his death.)

57.author: Arberry
57.text: We raised him up to a high place.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: And We exalted him to a position lofty. *Chapter: 19

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: And We raised him to a high station.

57.author: Itani
57.text: And We raised him to a high position.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: and We exalted him to a lofty position.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: And We raised him to a high station.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: And We raised him to high station.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: and We raised him to an exalted station.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: We raised him to a high place.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: And We raised him to a high station.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: We granted him a high position.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: And We raised him high in Heaven.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: We raised him to a high position.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: And We raised him to a lofty station.

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: These are (some of) those who were favoured by God among the prophets of the progeny of Adam, and of those We bore in the ark with Noah, and the offspring of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and We chose, for they bowed weeping in adoration when the revelations of Ar-Rahman were recited to them.

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: It is these upon whom Allah has bestowed favour among the Prophets, from the descendants of Adam; and from those whom We boarded along with Nooh; and from the descendants of Ibrahim and Israel; and from those whom We guided and chose; when the verses of the Most Gracious were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating and weeping. (* Command of Prostration # 5.)

58.author: Arberry
58.text: These are they whom God has blessed among the Prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those We bore with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: These are they whom Allah hath favoured, from among the prophets, of the progeny of Adam and of them whom We bare with Nuh, and of the progeny of Ibrahim and Isra'il, and of those whom We have guided and chosen; whenever the revelations of the Compassionate were rehearsed unto them, they fell down prostrating themselves and weeping. *Chapter: 19

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His Grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh (Noah), and of the offspring of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Israel and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Verses of the Most Beneficent (Allah) were recited unto them, they fell down prostrating and weeping.

58.author: Itani
58.text: These are some of the prophets God has blessed, from the descendants of Adam, and from those We carried with Noah, and from the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and from those We guided and selected. Whenever the revelations of the Most Gracious are recited to them, they would fall down, prostrating and weeping.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: These are the Prophets upon whom Allah bestowed His favour from the seed of Adam, and from the seed of those whom We carried (in the Ark) with Noah, and from the seed of Abraham and Israel. They were those whom We guided and chose (for an exalted position). They were such that when the words of the Most Compassionate Lord were recited to them, they fell down in prostration, weeping.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh, and of the offspring of Ibrahim and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Ayat of the Most Gracious were recited unto them, they fell down prostrate and weeping.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: These are they unto whom Allah showed favour from among the prophets, of the seed of Adam and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the revelations of the Beneficent were recited unto them, they fell down, adoring and weeping.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: They are the ones whom Allah has blessed from among the prophets of Adam’s progeny, and from [the progeny of] those We carried with Noah, and from among the progeny of Abraham and Israel, and from among those that We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-beneficent were recited to them, they would fall down weeping in prostration.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: These are whom Allah has blessed among the Prophets from among the seed of Adam and of those whom We bore with Noah; the descendants ofAbraham, of Israel, and of those whom We have guided and chose. For when the verses of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: Those were the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: These were the Prophets from the offspring of Adam, from those who embarked with Noah and from the offspring of Abraham and Israel. God guided them and chose them for His favor. Whenever they would hear the revelations of the Beneficent God they would bow down in prostration with tears.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: These are they on whom Allah bestowed favors, from among the prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those whom We carried with Nuh, and of the seed of Ibrahim and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose; when the communications of the Beneficent Allah were recited to them, they fell down making obeisance and weeping.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: These are the ones whom God has favoured: the prophets from among the descendants of Adam and of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah; the descendants of Abraham, of Israel, and of those whom We have guided and chosen. For when the revelations of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating themselves and weeping.

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: Those were some of the prophets on whom Allah did bestow His Grace,- of the posterity of Adam, and of those who We carried (in the Ark) with Noah, and of the posterity of Abraham and Israel of those whom We guided and chose. Whenever the Signs of (Allah) Most Gracious were rehearsed to them, they would fall down in prostrate adoration and in tears.

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Olaika allatheena anAAama Allahu AAalayhim mina alnnabiyyeena min thurriyyati adama wamimman hamalna maAAa noohin wamin thurriyyati ibraheema waisraeela wamimman hadayna waijtabayna itha tutla AAalayhim ayatu alrrahmani kharroo sujjadan wabukiyyan

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: But they are succeeded by a generation who neglect their devotional obligations and follow only earthly pleasures; but they will reach the wrong road and meet destruction,

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: And after them came the unworthy successors who squandered prayer and pursued their own desires, so they will soon encounter the forest of Gai in hell.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: Then there succeeded after them a succession who wasted the prayer, and followed lusts; so they shall encounter error

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Then there succeeded to them a posterity who neglected the prayers and followed lusts; so presently they shall meet with perdition. *Chapter: 19

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Then, there has succeeded them a posterity who have given up As-Salat (the prayers) [i.e. made their Salat (prayers) to be lost, either by not offering them or by not offering them perfectly or by not offering them in their proper fixed times, etc.] and have followed lusts. So they will be thrown in Hell.

59.author: Itani
59.text: But they were succeeded by generations who lost the prayers and followed their appetites. They will meet perdition.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: They were succeeded by a people who neglected the Prayers and pursued their lusts. They shall presently meet with their doom,

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Then, there has succeeded them a posterity who have lost the Salah and have followed lusts. So they will meet Ghaiy.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Now there hath succeeded them a later generation whom have ruined worship and have followed lusts. But they will meet deception.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: But they were succeeded by an evil posterity who neglected the prayer, and followed [their base] appetites. So they will soon encounter [the reward of] perversity,

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: But the generation that succeeded them wasted their prayers and followed their desires, so they shall encounter error

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: But there came after them successors who neglected prayer and pursued desires; so they are going to meet evil -

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: They were succeeded by a generation who neglected their prayers and followed their worldly desires. They will certainly be lost,

59.author: Shakir
59.text: But there came after them an evil generation, who neglected prayers and followed and sensual desires, so they win meet perdition,

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: But then they were succeeded by generations who neglected their prayers and were driven by their own desires. They will assuredly meet with destruction,

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: But after them there followed a posterity who missed prayers and followed after lusts soon, then, will they face Destruction,-

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Fakhalafa min baAAdihim khalfun adaAAoo alssalata waittabaAAoo alshshahawati fasawfa yalqawna ghayyan

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: Except those who repent and come to believe and do the right. These will enter Paradise and will not be wronged the least

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: Except those who repented and accepted faith and did good deeds – so these will enter heaven, and they will not be deprived* in the least. (* Of their due reward.)

60.author: Arberry
60.text: save him who repents, and believes, and does a righteous deed; those -- they shall enter Paradise, and they shall not be wronged anything;

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: Excepting those who may repent and believe and work righteously; these shall enter the Garden and shall not be wronged at all *Chapter: 19

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: Except those who repent and believe (in the Oneness of Allah and His Messenger Muhammad SAW), and work righteousness. Such will enter Paradise and they will not be wronged in aught.

60.author: Itani
60.text: Except for those who repent, and believe, and act righteously. These will enter Paradise, and will not be wronged in the least.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: except those who repent and believe and act righteously. Such shall enter Paradise and shall not be wronged at all.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: Except those who repent and believe, and work righteousness. Such will enter Paradise and they will not be wronged in aught.

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: Save him who shall repent and believe and do right. Such will enter the Garden, and they will not be wronged in aught -

60.author: Qarai
60.text: barring those who repent, believe, and act righteously. Such will enter paradise, and they will not be wronged in the least.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: except he who repents and believes and does good works; those shall be admitted to Paradise and shall not be wronged in any way.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: Except those who repent, believe and do righteousness; for those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged at all.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: but those among them who repent and become righteously striving believers will be admitted to the gardens without experiencing any injustice.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: Except such as repent and believe and do good, these shall enter the garden, and they shall not be dealt with unjustly in any way:

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: except for those who repent and believe and do good deeds. These will enter Heaven, and they will not be wronged in the least.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: Except those who repent and believe, and work righteousness: for these will enter the Garden and will not be wronged in the least,-

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila salihan faolaika yadkhuloona aljannata wala yuthlamoona shayan

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: In the gardens of Eden promised by Ar-Rahman to His creatures in the unknown (future). Verily His promise will come to pass.

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: Everlasting Gardens of Eden, which the Most Gracious has promised to His bondmen in the unseen; indeed His promise will come.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: Gardens of Eden that the All-merciful promised His servants in the Unseen; His promise is ever performed.

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Gardens Everlasting, which the Compassionate hath promised unto his bondmen, unseen; verily His premise is ever to be fulfilled. *Chapter: 19

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: (They will enter) 'Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), which the Most Beneficent (Allah) has promised to His slaves in the unseen: Verily! His Promise must come to pass.

61.author: Itani
61.text: The Gardens of Eden, promised by the Most Merciful to His servants in the Unseen. His promise will certainly come true.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: Theirs shall be everlasting Gardens which the Most Compassionate Lord has promised His servants in a realm which is beyond the ken of perception. Surely His promise shall he fulfilled.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: (They will enter) `Adn Gardens which the Most Gracious has promised to His servants in the unseen. Verily, His promise must come to pass.

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Gardens of Eden, which the Beneficent hath promised to His slaves in the unseen. Lo! His promise is ever sure of fulfilment -

61.author: Qarai
61.text: Gardens of Eden promised by the All-beneficent to His servants, [while they were still] unseen. Indeed His promise is bound to come to pass.

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: (They shall enter the) Gardens of Eden, which the Merciful has promised His worshipers in the Unseen. Indeed, His promise shall come.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: [Therein are] gardens of perpetual residence which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen. Indeed, His promise has ever been coming.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: They will be admitted to the garden of Eden which is the unseen promise of the Beneficent God to His servants. The promise of God will certainly come true.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: The gardens of perpetuity which the Beneficent Allah has promised to His servants while unseen; surely His promise shall come to pass.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: Theirs shall be the Gardens of Eden, which the All Merciful has promised to His servants without their having seen them, and most surely His promise shall be fulfilled.

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: Gardens of Eternity, those which (Allah) Most Gracious has promised to His servants in the Unseen: for His promise must (necessarily) come to pass.

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Jannati AAadnin allatee waAAada alrrahmanu AAibadahu bialghaybi innahu kana waAAduhu matiyyan

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: They will hear no vain talk there, but only salutations of peace, and they will have their sustenance morning and evening."

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: They will not hear any lewd talk in it, but only Peace; and in it for them is sustenance, every morning and evening.

62.author: Arberry
62.text: There they shall hear no idle talk, but only 'Peace.' There they shall have their provision at dawn and evening.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: They shall not hear therein any vain word, but they shall hear only peace; and therein they shall have their provision morning and evening. *Chapter: 19

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: They shall not hear therein (in Paradise) any Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk), but only Salam (salutations of peace). And they will have therein their sustenance, morning and afternoon. [See (V. 40:55)].

62.author: Itani
62.text: They will hear no nonsense therein, but only peace. And they will have their provision therein, morning and evening.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: They shall not hear in it anything vain; they shall hear only what is good; and they shall have their provision in it, morning and evening.

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: They shall not hear therein any Laghw, but only Salam. And they will have therein their sustenance, morning and afternoon.

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: They hear therein no idle talk, but only Peace; and therein they have food for morn and evening.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: Therein they will not hear vain talk, but only ‘Peace!’ Therein they will have their provision morning and evening.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: There, they shall hear no idle talk, but only peace. And there they shall be given their provision at dawn and at the evening.

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: They will not hear therein any ill speech - only [greetings of] peace - and they will have their provision therein, morning and afternoon.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: They will not hear therein any meaningless words. They will be greeted (by the angels) with "Peace be with you," and they will receive their sustenance both in the mornings and evenings.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: They shall not hear therein any vain discourse, but only: Peace, and they shall have their sustenance therein morning and evening.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: They will not hear therein anything vain, only greetings of peace. They will receive their provision there morning and evening.

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: They will not there hear any vain discourse, but only salutations of Peace: And they will have therein their sustenance, morning and evening.

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: La yasmaAAoona feeha laghwan illa salaman walahum rizquhum feeha bukratan waAAashiyyan

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: This is the Paradise those of Our creatures will inherit who take heed and fear the displeasure of God.

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: It is the Paradise that We will bequeath to those among Our bondmen who remain pious.

63.author: Arberry
63.text: That is Paradise which We shall give as an inheritance to those of Our servants who are godfearing.

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Such is the Garden which We shall cause those of Our bondmen to inherit who have been God-fearing. *Chapter: 19

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Such is the Paradise which We shall give as an inheritance to those of Our slaves who have been Al-Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - See V. 2:2).

63.author: Itani
63.text: Such is Paradise which We will give as inheritance to those of Our servants who are devout.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Such is the Paradise which We shall cause those of Our servants who have been God-fearing to inherit.

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: Such is the Paradise which We shall give as an inheritance to those of Our servants who had Taqwa.

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Such is the Garden which We cause the devout among Our bondmen to inherit.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: This is the paradise We will give as inheritance to those of Our servants who are Godwary.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: That is the Paradise which We shall give the cautious worshipers to inherit.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: That is Paradise, which We give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were fearing of Allah.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: Such are the gardens which We will give to Our God-fearing servants as their inherited property.

63.author: Shakir
63.text: This is the garden which We cause those of Our servants to inherit who guard (against evil).

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: That is the Garden which We will grant to those of Our servants who have been God-fearing.

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Such is the Garden which We give as an inheritance to those of Our servants who guard against Evil.

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Tilka aljannatu allatee noorithu min AAibadina man kana taqiyyan

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: "We do not come down," (will the angels say) "but only by your Lord's command." To Him belongs whatever lies before us and behind us, and the space in between. Your Lord does not ever forget:

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: (Said Angel Jibreel to Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon them) “And we angels do not come down except by the command of your Lord; to Him only belongs all that is ahead of us and all that is behind us and all that is between them; and your Lord is not forgetful.”

64.author: Arberry
64.text: We come not down, save at the commandment of thy Lord. To Him belongs all that is before us, and all that is behind us, and all between that.

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: And we descend not except by the command of thy Lord: his is whatsoever is before us and whatsoever is behind us and whatsoever is in-between; and thy Lord is not a forgetter *Chapter: 19

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: And we (angels) descend not except by the Command of your Lord (O Muhammad SAW). To Him belongs what is before us and what is behind us, and what is between those two, and your Lord is never forgetful,

64.author: Itani
64.text: “We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. His is what is before us, and what is behind us, and what is between them. Your Lord is never forgetful.”

64.author: Maududi
64.text: (The angels will say): "(O Muhammad!) We descend not except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs all that is before us and all that is behind us, and all that is in between. Your Lord is not forgetful in the least.

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: And we descend not except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs what is before us and what is behind us, and what is between those two; and your Lord is never forgetful.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: We (angels) come not down save by commandment of thy Lord. Unto Him belongeth all that is before us and all that is behind us and all that is between those two, and thy Lord was never forgetful -

64.author: Qarai
64.text: [O Gabriel, tell the Prophet,] ‘We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is in between that, and your Lord does not forget

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: (Gabriel said:) 'We do not descend except at the command of your Lord. To Him belongs all that is before us and all that is behind us, and all that lies between. Your Lord does not forget.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: [Gabriel said], "And we [angels] descend not except by the order of your Lord. To Him belongs that before us and that behind us and what is in between. And never is your Lord forgetful -

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: (Muhammad), we (the angels) do not come to you without being commanded by your Lord to do so. To Him belongs all that is before us, behind us, and in between. Your Lord is not forgetful.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: And we do not descend but by the command of your Lord; to Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is between these, and your Lord is not forgetful.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: We never descend except at your Lord's command. What is before us and behind us and all that lies between belong to Him. Your Lord is not forgetful.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: (The angels say:) "We descend not but by command of thy Lord: to Him belongeth what is before us and what is behind us, and what is between: and thy Lord never doth forget,-

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Wama natanazzalu illa biamri rabbika lahu ma bayna aydeena wama khalfana wama bayna thalika wama kana rabbuka nasiyyan

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them. Therefore worship Him, and be constant in His worship. Do you know any namesake of His?

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them – therefore worship Him and be firm in His worship; do you know any other of the same name as His?

65.author: Arberry
65.text: And thy Lord is never forgetful, Lord He of the heavens and earth and all that is between them. So serve Him, and be thou patient in His service; knowest thou any that can be named with His Name?

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that which is betwixt the twain; so Him worship thou, and endure patiently in His worship; knowest thou any as his compeer? *Chapter: 19

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, so worship Him (Alone) and be constant and patient in His worship. Do you know of any who is similar to Him? (of course none is similar or coequal or comparable to Him, and He has none as partner with Him). [There is nothing like unto Him and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer].

65.author: Itani
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them. So worship Him, and persevere in His service. Do you know of anyone equal to Him?

65.author: Maududi
65.text: He is the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is in between. Serve Him, then, and be constant in serving Him. Do you know anyone that might be His compeer?"

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, so worship Him and abide patiently in His worship. Do you know of any who is similar to Him

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them! Therefor, worship thou Him and be thou steadfast in His service. Knowest thou one that can be named along with Him?

65.author: Qarai
65.text: —the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. So worship Him and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know anyone who could be His namesake?’

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, so worship Him, be patient in His worship. Do you know any that can be named with His Name'

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them - so worship Him and have patience for His worship. Do you know of any similarity to Him?"

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: He is the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them. Worship Him and be steadfast in your worship of Him; none is equal to Him.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, so serve Him and be patient in His service. Do you know any one equal to Him?

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: He is the Lord of the heavens and of the earth and of all that is between the two. So worship Him alone and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know of anyone equal to Him in His attributes?

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: "Lord of the heavens and of the earth, and of all that is between them; so worship Him, and be constant and patient in His worship: knowest thou of any who is worthy of the same Name as He?"

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma faoAAbudhu waistabir liAAibadatihi hal taAAlamu lahu samiyyan

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: Yet man says: "When I am dead, will I come to life again?"

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: And says man, “When I am dead, will I soon be brought forth alive?”

66.author: Arberry
66.text: Man says, 'What, when I am dead shall I then be brought forth alive?'

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: And man saith: when I am daed, Shall I be presently brought forth alive. *Chapter: 19

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: And man (the disbeliever) says: "When I am dead, shall I then be raised up alive?"

66.author: Itani
66.text: And the human being says, “When I am dead, will I be brought back alive?”

66.author: Maududi
