66.text: Man is prone to say: "Shall I be raised to life after I die?"

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: And man says: "When I am dead, shall I then be raised up alive"

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: And man saith: When I am dead, shall I forsooth be brought forth alive?

66.author: Qarai
66.text: Man says, ‘What? Shall I be brought forth alive [from the grave], when I have been dead?’

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: The human says: 'What, when I am dead, shall I be raised to life'

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: And the disbeliever says, "When I have died, am I going to be brought forth alive?"

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: The human being says, "Shall I be brought to life again after I will die?"

66.author: Shakir
66.text: And says man: What! when I am dead shall I truly be brought forth alive?

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Man asks, "When I am once dead, shall I be raised to life?"

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: Man says: "What! When I am dead, shall I then be raised up alive?"

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Wayaqoolu alinsanu aitha ma mittu lasawfa okhraju hayyan

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Does man not remember that before We created him he was nothing?

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: Does not man remember that We created him before this, and he was non existent?

67.author: Arberry
67.text: Will not man remember that We created him aforetime, when he was nothing?

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Remembereth not man that We created him afore when he was not aught? *Chapter: 19

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: Does not man remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?

67.author: Itani
67.text: Does the human being not remember that We created him before, when he was nothing?

67.author: Maududi
67.text: Does man not remember that We created him before when he was nothing?

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: Does not man remember that We created him before, while he was nothing

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Doth not man remember that We created him before, when he was naught?

67.author: Qarai
67.text: Does not man remember that We created him before when he was nothing?

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: Will the human not remember that We created him before when he was not a thing.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: Does man not remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: Does he not remember that We created him when he did not exist?

67.author: Shakir
67.text: Does not man remember that We created him before, when he was nothing?

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: But does man not remember that We created him when he was nothing before?

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: But does not man call to mind that We created him before out of nothing?

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Awala yathkuru alinsanu anna khalaqnahu min qablu walam yaku shayan

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: By your Lord, We shall gather them and the devils together, then bring them crawling on their knees around Hell.

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: So by your Lord, We shall assemble them and the devils – all of them – and bring them around hell, fallen on their knees.

68.author: Arberry
68.text: Now, by thy Lord, We shall surely muster them, and the Satans, then We shall parade them about Gehenna hobbling on their knees.

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: By thy Lord, then, We shall surely gather them and the satans; thereafter We shall surely bring them round Hell, kneeling. *Chapter: 19

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: So by your Lord, surely, We shall gather them together, and (also) the Shayatin (devils) (with them), then We shall bring them round Hell on their knees.

68.author: Itani
68.text: By your Lord, We will round them up, and the devils, then We will bring them around Hell, on their knees.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: By your Lord, We will surely muster them and the devils together. Then We will surely bring them all, on their knees, around Hell,

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: So by your Lord, surely We shall gather them together, and (also) the Shayatin, then We shall bring them round Hell, Jithyya.

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: And, by thy Lord, verily We shall assemble them and the devils, then We shall bring them, crouching, around hell.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: By your Lord, We will surely gather them with the devils; then We will surely bring them up around hell [scrambling] on their knees.

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: By your Lord, We will gather them and the satans, and We shall parade them hobbling on their knees in Gehenna:

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: So by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils; then We will bring them to be present around Hell upon their knees.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: By your Lord, We will bring them back to life with satan and gather them around hell in large groups.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: So by your Lord! We will most certainly gather them together and the Shaitans, then shall We certainly cause them to be present round hell on their knees.

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: By your Lord, We shall most surely gather them and the devils too; and bring them close to hell on their knees.

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: So, by thy Lord, without doubt, We shall gather them together, and (also) the Evil Ones (with them); then shall We bring them forth on their knees round about Hell;

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Fawarabbika lanahshurannahum waalshshayateena thumma lanuhdirannahum hawla jahannama jithiyyan

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: We shall pull out of every section those who were most perversely rebellious against Ar-Rahman.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: We shall then pick out from every group the one who was most arrogant towards the Most Gracious.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: Then We shall pluck forth from every party whichever of them was the most hardened in disdain of the All-merciful;

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: Thereafter, We shall surely draw aside, from each sect, whichever of them against the Compassionate were most in excess. *Chapter: 19

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: Then indeed We shall drag out from every sect all those who were worst in obstinate rebellion against the Most Beneficent (Allah).

69.author: Itani
69.text: Then, out of every sect, We will snatch those most defiant to the Most Merciful.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: and then We will draw aside from each party those who were most rebellious against the Most Compassionate Lord,

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: Then indeed We shall drag out from every sect all those who were worst in obstinate rebellion against the Most Gracious.

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: Then We shall pluck out from every sect whichever of them was most stubborn in rebellion to the Beneficent.

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Then from every group We shall draw whichever of them was more defiant towards the All-beneficent.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: from every party We will pluck out whichever of them was the most hardened in disdain of the Most Merciful.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Then We will surely extract from every sect those of them who were worst against the Most Merciful in insolence.

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Then We will separate from every group those who were strongly rebellious against the Beneficent God.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: Then We will most certainly draw forth from every sect of them him who is most exorbitantly rebellious against the Beneficent Allah.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Then We shall carry off from every group those who were most stubborn in their opposition to the Gracious One,

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: Then shall We certainly drag out from every sect all those who were worst in obstinate rebellion against (Allah) Most Gracious.

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Thumma lananziAAanna min kulli sheeAAatin ayyuhum ashaddu AAala alrrahmani AAitiyyan

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: We know best who deserve to be burnt in (the Fire).

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: Moreover, We well know those who most deserve to be burned in hell.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: then We shall know very well those most deserving to burn there.

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: Then surely it is We who are the Best Knower of these worthiest of being therein roasted. *Chapter: 19

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: Then, verily, We know best those who are most worthy of being burnt therein.

70.author: Itani
70.text: We are fully aware of those most deserving to scorch in it.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: and then We shall know well all those most worthy to be cast in Hell.

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: Then, verily, We know best those who are most worthy of being burnt therein.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: And surely We are Best Aware of those most worthy to be burned therein.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: Then surely We will know best those who deserve most to enter it.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: We alone know who deserves most to be burned therein.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: Then, surely it is We who are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: We know best who deserves greater suffering in hell fire.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: Again We do certainly know best those who deserve most to be burned therein.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: We surely know best those most deserving of the fires of hell,

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: And certainly We know best those who are most worthy of being burned therein.

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Thumma lanahnu aAAlamu biallatheena hum awla biha siliyyan

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: There is not one among you who will not reach it. Your Lord has made this incumbent on Himself.

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: And there is none among you who shall not pass over hell; this is an obligatory affair, binding upon your Lord. (Allah will make everyone pass over the back of hell – on a thin bridge.)

71.author: Arberry
71.text: Not one of you there is, but he shall go down to it; that for thy Lord is a thing decreed, determined.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: And not one of you but shall pass over it: it is for thy Lord an ordinance decreed. *Chapter: 19

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: There is not one of you but will pass over it (Hell); this is with your Lord; a Decree which must be accomplished.

71.author: Itani
71.text: There is not one of you but will go down to it. This has been an unavoidable decree of your Lord.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: There is not one of you but shall pass by Hell. This is a decree which your Lord will fulfil.

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: There is not one of you but will pass over it (Hell); this is with your Lord, a decree which must be accomplished.

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: There is not one of you but shall approach it. That is a fixed ordinance of thy Lord.

71.author: Qarai
71.text: There is none of you but will come to it: a [matter that is a] decided certainty with your Lord.

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: There is not one of you who shall not go down to it: such is a thing decreed, determined by your Lord.

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: And there is none of you except he will come to it. This is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: It is the inevitable decree of your Lord that every one of you will be taken to hell.

71.author: Shakir
71.text: And there is not one of you but shall come to it; this is an unavoidable decree of your Lord.

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: and there is not one of you but shall pass through it: a decree from your Lord which must be fulfilled.

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: Not one of you but will pass over it: this is, with thy Lord, a Decree which must be accomplished.

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Wain minkum illa wariduha kana AAala rabbika hatman maqdiyyan

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: We shall deliver those who took heed for themselves, and leave the evil-doers kneeling there.

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: We shall then rescue the pious – and leave the unjust in it, fallen on their knees.

72.author: Arberry
72.text: Then We shall deliver those that were godfearing; and the evildoers We shall leave there, hobbling on their knees.

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Then We shall deliver those who have feared, and shall have the wrong-doers therein kneeling. *Chapter: 19

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: Then We shall save those who use to fear Allah and were dutiful to Him. And We shall leave the Zalimun (polytheists and wrongdoers, etc.) therein (humbled) to their knees (in Hell).

72.author: Itani
72.text: Then We will rescue those who were devout, and leave the wrongdoers in it, on their knees.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: Then We shall deliver those that feared Allah and leave the wrong-doers there on their knees.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: Then We shall save those who had Taqwa. And We shall leave the wrongdoers in it, Jithiyya.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: Then We shall rescue those who kept from evil, and leave the evil-doers crouching there.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: Then We will deliver those who are Godwary, and leave the wrongdoers in it, fallen on their knees.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: Then, We will save those who were cautious of Us, but the harmdoers shall be left there hobbling on their knees.

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: Then We will save those who feared Allah and leave the wrongdoers within it, on their knees.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: We will save the pious ones from the hell fire and leave the unjust people therein in crowded groups.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: And We will deliver those who guarded (against evil), and We will leave the unjust therein on their knees.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: Then We shall save those who feared God, but the wrongdoers shall be left there on their knees.

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: But We shall save those who guarded against evil, and We shall leave the wrong-doers therein, (humbled) to their knees.

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Thumma nunajjee allatheena ittaqaw wanatharu alththalimeena feeha jithiyyan

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: When Our lucid revelations are read out to them, the infidels say to those who believe: "Which of the two groups is better in standing, and whose company is more excellent?"

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: And when Our clear verses are recited to them, the disbelievers say to the Muslims, “Which group has a better home, and a better alliance?”

73.author: Arberry
73.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, the unbelievers say to the believers, 'Which of the two parties is better in station, fairer in assembly?'

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: And when Our manifest revelations are rehearsed unto them, those who disbelieve say unto those who believe: which of the two portions is better in station and goodlier in company? *Chapter: 19

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, those who disbelieve (the rich and strong among the pagans of Quraish who live a life of luxury) say to those who believe (the weak, poor companions of Prophet Muhammad SAW who have a hard life): "Which of the two groups (i.e. believers and disbelievers) is best in (point of) position and as regards station (place of council for consultation)."

73.author: Itani
73.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Which of the two parties is better in position, and superior in influence?”

73.author: Maududi
73.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to those who deny the Truth they are wont to say to those who have faith: "Which of the two groups has a better status and whose assemblies are grander?"

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: And when Our clear Ayat are recited to them, those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Which of the two groups has the best dwellings and the finest Nadiyyan"

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: And when Our clear revelations are recited unto them, those who disbelieve say unto those who believe: Which of the two parties (yours or ours) is better in position, and more imposing as an army?

73.author: Qarai
73.text: When Our manifest signs are recited to them, the faithless say to the faithful, ‘Which of the two groups is superior in station and better with respect to company?’

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: When Our clear verses are recited to them the unbelievers say to the believers: 'Which of the two parties has a better position or company'

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Which of [our] two parties is best in position and best in association?"

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: When Our revelations are recited to them, the unbelievers say to the faithful ones, "Which of us is more prosperous?"

73.author: Shakir
73.text: And when Our clear communications are recited to them, those who disbelieve say to those who believe: Which of the two parties is best in abiding and best in assembly?

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, those who deny the truth say to the faithful, "Which of the two sides is better in respect of position and makes a more impressive assembly?"

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: When Our Clear Signs are rehearsed to them, the Unbelievers say to those who believe, "Which of the two sides is best in point of position? Which makes the best show in council?"

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: Waitha tutla AAalayhim ayatuna bayyinatin qala allatheena kafaroo lillatheena amanoo ayyu alfareeqayni khayrun maqaman waahsanu nadiyyan

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: How many generations that had far more wealth and ostentation have We laid low before them!

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: And many a generation We did destroy before them, who exceeded them in wealth and pomp!

74.author: Arberry
74.text: And how many a generation We destroyed before them, who were fairer in furnishing and outward show!

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before these, who were goodlier in goods and outward appearance? *Chapter: 19

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: And how many a generation (past nations) have We destroyed before them, who were better in wealth, goods and outward appearance?

74.author: Itani
74.text: How many a generation have We destroyed before them, who surpassed them in riches and splendor?

74.author: Maududi
74.text: How numerous are the peoples We destroyed before them - those that were more resourceful and grander in outward appearance!

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them, who were better in wealth, goods and outward appearance

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: How many a generation have We destroyed before them, who were more imposing in respect of gear and outward seeming!

74.author: Qarai
74.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them, who were superior in furnishings and appearance!

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them, who were far greater in riches and more boastful!

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them who were better in possessions and [outward] appearance?

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: How many generations of greater prosperity and splendor have We destroyed before them?

74.author: Shakir
74.text: And how many of the generations have We destroyed before them who were better in respect of goods and outward appearance!

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: We have destroyed so many generations before them, who surpassed them in material power and splendour.

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: But how many (countless) generations before them have we destroyed, who were even better in equipment and in glitter to the eye?

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hum ahsanu athathan wariyan

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: Say: "Ar-Rahman extends the life of those who are astray until they come to realise what had been promised them was either (physical) affliction or (the terror) of Resurrection. Then will they know who is worse in position, and who is weak in supporters.

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: Proclaim, “For one in error – so the Most Gracious may give him respite; to the extent that when they see the thing which they are promised – either the punishment or the Last Day; so then they will come to know for whom is the evil rank and whose army is weak.”

75.author: Arberry
75.text: Say: 'Whosoever is in error, let the All-merciful prolong his term for him! Till, when they see that they were threatened, whether the chastisement, or the Hour, then they shall surely know who is worse in place, and who is weaker in hosts.'

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: Say thou: whosoever is in error--surely unto him the Compassionate lengtheneth a length, until When they behold that wherewith they were threatened, either the torment or the Hour, then they shall come to know whosoever is worse in position and weaker as an army. *Chapter: 19

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW) whoever is in error, the Most Beneficent (Allah) will extend (the rope) to him, until, when they see that which they were promised, either the torment or the Hour, they will come to know who is worst in position, and who is weaker in forces. [This is the answer for the Verse No. 19:73]

75.author: Itani
75.text: Say, “Whoever is in error, the Most Merciful will lead him on.” Until, when they see what they were promised—either the punishment, or the Hour. Then they will know who was in worse position and weaker in forces.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: Say: "The Most Compassionate Lord grants respite to those who stray into error, until they behold what they had been threatened with, either God's chastisement (in the world) or the Hour (of Resurrection)" - then they fully know whose station is worse, and who is weaker in hosts!

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: Say: whoever is in error, the Most Gracious will extend [circumstances] for him, until, when they see that which they were promised, either the torment or the Hour, they will come to know who is worst in position, and who is weaker in forces.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: Say: As for him who is in error, the Beneficent will verily prolong his span of life until, when they behold that which they were promised, whether it be punishment (in the world), or the Hour (of doom), they will know who is worse in position and who is weaker as an army.

75.author: Qarai
75.text: Say, ‘Whoever abides in error, the All-beneficent shall prolong his respite until they sight what they have been promised: either punishment, or the Hour.’ Then they will know whose position is worse, and whose host is weaker.

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: Say: 'Whosoever is in error, let the Most Merciful prolong his life span until they see that which they were threatened, be it a punishment, or the Hour. Thenshall they know whose is the worst place and who is weaker in hosts'

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: Say, "Whoever is in error - let the Most Merciful extend for him an extension [in wealth and time] until, when they see that which they were promised - either punishment [in this world] or the Hour [of resurrection] - they will come to know who is worst in position and weaker in soldiers."

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "The Beneficent God gives respite to those who have gone astray only until they face the torment with which they were threatened or to the Day of Judgment. Then they will find out who will have the most miserable place and the weakest forces.

75.author: Shakir
75.text: Say: As for him who remains in error, the Beneficent Allah will surely prolong his length of days, until they see what they were threatened with, either the punishment or the hour; then they shall know who is in more evil plight and weaker in forces

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: Say, "The Gracious One grants respite for a time to those who are in error until, when they are confronted with what they are promised, either in punishment [in the world] or in [the approach of] the Hour, they will realize who is worse in respect of position and who is weaker in resources."

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: Say: "If any men go astray, (Allah) Most Gracious extends (the rope) to them, until, when they see the warning of Allah (being fulfilled) - either in punishment or in (the approach of) the Hour,- they will at length realise who is worst in position, and (who) weakest in forces!

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Qul man kana fee alddalalati falyamdud lahu alrrahmanu maddan hatta itha raaw ma yooAAadoona imma alAAathaba waimma alssaAAata fasayaAAlamoona man huwa sharrun makanan waadAAafu jundan

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: God gives greater guidance to those who are guided; and good deeds that endure are better with your Lord for reward, and better for consequence.

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: And Allah will increase the guidance for those who have received guidance; and good deeds that remain have the best reward before your Lord, and the best outcome.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: And God shall increase those who were guided in guidance; and the abiding things, the deeds of righteousness, are better with thy Lord in reward, and better in return

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: And Allah increaseth in guidance those who let themselves be guided, and the righteous works that last are excellent with thy Lord in respect of reward and excellent in respect of return. *Chapter: 19

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: And Allah increases in guidance those who walk aright [true believers in the Oneness of Allah who fear Allah much (abstain from all kinds of sins and evil deeds which He has forbidden), and love Allah much (perform all kinds of good deeds which He has ordained)]. And the righteous good deeds that last, are better with your Lord, for reward and better for resort.

76.author: Itani
76.text: God increases in guidance those who accept guidance. And the things that endure—the righteous deeds—have the best reward with your Lord, and the best outcome.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: (On the contrary), Allah increases in guidance those who follow the Right Way. Lasting acts of righteousness are better in the sight of your Lord as reward and conducive to a better end.

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: And Allah increases in guidance those who walk aright. And the righteous good deeds that last are better with your Lord for reward and better for resort.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: Allah increaseth in right guidance those who walk aright, and the good deeds which endure are better in thy Lord's sight for reward, and better for resort.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: Allah enhances in guidance those who are [rightly] guided, and lasting righteous deeds are better with your Lord in reward, and better at the return [to Allah].

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: Allah will increase those who were guided in guidance and things that abide. Good deeds are better in reward with your Lord, and, better in return.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: And Allah increases those who were guided, in guidance, and the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for recourse.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: God further enlightens those who seek guidance. To those who do charitable deeds which produce continuing benefits, your Lord will give a better reward and a better place in Paradise.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: And Allah increases in guidance those who go aright; and ever-abiding good works are with your Lord best in recompense and best in yielding fruit.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: God increases His guidance to those who follow guidance; and lasting good works are better in the sight of your Lord and are most rewarding.

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: "And Allah doth advance in guidance those who seek guidance: and the things that endure, Good Deeds, are best in the sight of thy Lord, as rewards, and best in respect of (their) eventual return."

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Wayazeedu Allahu allatheena ihtadaw hudan waalbaqiyatu alssalihatu khayrun AAinda rabbika thawaban wakhayrun maraddan

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: Have you seen him who denies Our revelations, and says: "I will certainly be given wealth and children."

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: So have you seen him who denied Our signs and says, “I shall certainly be given wealth and children?”

77.author: Arberry
77.text: Hast thou seen him who disbelieves in Our signs and says, 'Assuredly I shall be given wealth and children'?

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Hast thou observed him who disbelieveth in Our signs and saith: surely I shall be vouchsafed riches and children. *Chapter: 19

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: Have you seen him who disbelieved in Our Ayat (this Quran and Muhammad SAW) and (yet) says: "I shall certainly be given wealth and children [if I will be alive (again)],"

77.author: Itani
77.text: Have you seen him who denied Our revelations, and said, “I will be given wealth and children”?

77.author: Maududi
77.text: Have you seen him who rejected Our signs and said: "Surely I shall continue to be favoured with riches and children."

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: Have you seen him who disbelieved in Our Ayat and said: "I shall certainly be given wealth and children [if I live again]."

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: Hast thou seen him who disbelieveth in Our revelations and saith: Assuredly I shall be given wealth and children?

77.author: Qarai
77.text: Have you not regarded him who defies Our signs, and says, ‘I will surely be given wealth and children’?

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: Have you seen he who disbelieves Our verses and yet says: 'I shall surely be given wealth and children'

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: Then, have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, "I will surely be given wealth and children [in the next life]?"

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: Note the words of the disbeliever, "I shall certainly be given wealth and children?"

77.author: Shakir
77.text: Have you, then, seen him who disbelieves in Our communications and says: I shall certainly be given wealth and children?

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: Have you seen him who denies the truth in Our revelations and says, "I shall certainly be given wealth and children."

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: Hast thou then seen the (sort of) man who rejects Our Signs, yet says: "I shall certainly be given wealth and children?"

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Afaraayta allathee kafara biayatina waqala laootayanna malan wawaladan

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Has he peeped into the Unknown, or obtained a promise from Ar-Rahman?

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: Has he seen the Hidden, or has he made a pact with the Most Gracious?

78.author: Arberry
78.text: What, has he observed the Unseen, or taken a covenant with the All-merciful?

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: Hath he looked unto the Unseen, or hath he taken of the Compassionate a covenant? *Chapter: 19

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: Has he known the unseen or has he taken a covenant from the Most Beneficent (Allah)?

78.author: Itani
78.text: Did he look into the future, or did he receive a promise from the Most Merciful?

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Has he obtained knowledge of the Unseen, or has he taken a covenant with the Most Compassionate Lord?

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: Has he known the Unseen or has he taken a covenant from the Most Gracious

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Hath he perused the Unseen, or hath he made a pact with the Beneficent?

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Has he come to know the Unseen, or taken a promise from the All-beneficent?

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: Has he gained knowledge of the Unseen? Or taken a covenant with the Merciful?

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken from the Most Merciful a promise?

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: Has he the knowledge of the unseen or has the Beneficent God established such a binding agreement with Him?

78.author: Shakir
78.text: Has he gained knowledge of the unseen, or made a covenant with the Beneficent Allah?

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Has he looked to the unseen, or has he made a pledge to the Merciful One?

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: Has he penetrated to the Unseen, or has he taken a contract with (Allah) Most Gracious?

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: AttalaAAa alghayba ami ittakhatha AAinda alrrahmani AAahdan

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: Never so. We shall certainly write down what he says, and prolong the extent of his punishment.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: Never; We shall now record what he says and give him a prolonged punishment.

79.author: Arberry
79.text: No, indeed! We shall assuredly write down all that he says, and We shall prolong for him the chastisement;

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: By no means! We shall write down that which he saith; and We shall lengthen for him of the torment a length. *Chapter: 19

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: Nay! We shall record what he says, and We shall increase his torment (in the Hell);

79.author: Itani
79.text: No indeed! We will write what he says, and will keep extending the agony for him.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: By no means! We shall write down all what he says; and We shall greatly prolong his chastisement,

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: Nay, We shall record what he says, and We shall increase his torment;

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: Nay, but We shall record that which he saith and prolong for him a span of torment.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: No indeed! We will write down what he says, and We will prolong his punishment endlessly.

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: On the contrary, We will write down what he says and prolong the length of his punishment.

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: No! We will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: Absolutely not, We will record his words and prolong his punishment.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: By no means! We write down what he says, and We will lengthen to him the length of the chastisement

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: Indeed not. We shall record what he says and shall prolong the punishment for him.

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: Nay! We shall record what he says, and We shall add and add to his punishment.

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Kalla sanaktubu ma yaqoolu wanamuddu lahu mina alAAathabi maddan

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: All that he claims will revert to Us, and he will come before Us all alone.

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: And it is We only Who shall inherit what he says (belongs to him), and he will come to Us, alone.

80.author: Arberry
80.text: and We shall inherit from him that he says, and he shall come to Us alone.

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And We shall inherit from him that whereof he spake, and he shall come to us alone. *Chapter: 19

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: And We shall inherit from him (at his death) all that he talks of (i.e. wealth and children which We have bestowed upon him in this world), and he shall come to Us alone.

80.author: Itani
80.text: Then We will inherit from him what he speaks of, and he will come to Us alone.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: and We shall inherit all the resources and hosts of which he boasts, and he will come to Us all alone.

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And We shall inherit from him all that he speaks of, and he shall come to Us alone.

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: And We shall inherit from him that whereof he spake, and he will come unto Us, alone (without his wealth and children).

80.author: Qarai
80.text: We shall take over from him what he talks about, and he will come to Us alone.

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: We shall inherit that of which he speaks and he will come before Us alone.

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: All that he speaks of will belong to Us, and he will come into Our presence all alone.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And We will inherit of him what he says, and he shall come to Us alone.

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: We shall inherit all that he boasts of, and he will come to Us all alone.

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: To Us shall return all that he talks of and he shall appear before Us bare and alone.

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: They have taken other gods apart from God that they might be a strength to them.

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And they have chosen Gods besides Allah, so that they may provide them strength!

81.author: Arberry
81.text: And they have taken to them other gods apart from God, that they might be for them a might.

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And they have taken gods beside Allah that they might be unto them a glory. *Chapter: 19

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: And they have taken (for worship) aliha (gods) besides Allah, that they might give them honour, power and glory (and also protect them from Allah's Punishment etc.).

81.author: Itani
81.text: And they took, besides God, other gods, to be for them a source of strength.

81.author: Maududi
81.text: They have taken other gods instead of Allah that they may be a source of strength for them.

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And they have taken gods besides Allah, that they might give them honor, power and glory.

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And they have chosen (other) gods beside Allah that they may be a power for them.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: They have taken gods besides Allah that they may be a [source of] might to them.

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: And they have taken gods, other than Allah, that they might be a power from them.

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And they have taken besides Allah [false] deities that they would be for them [a source of] honor.

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: They have sought honor from other gods instead of God.

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And they have taken gods besides Allah, that they should be to them a source of strength;

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: They have taken other deities besides God, so that they may be a source of strength for them.

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: And they have taken (for worship) gods other than Allah, to give them power and glory!

81.author: Transliteration
81.text: Waittakhathoo min dooni Allahi alihatan liyakoonoo lahum AAizzan

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: Never. They will deny their devotion and become their adversaries.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: Never; soon they will deny ever worshipping them, and will turn into their opponents.

82.author: Arberry
82.text: No, indeed! They shall deny their service, and they shall be against them pitted.

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: By no means! anon they shall deny their worship, and become unto them an adversary. *Chapter: 19

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: Nay, but they (the so-called gods) will deny their worship of them, and become opponents to them (on the Day of Resurrection).

82.author: Itani
82.text: By no means! They will reject their worship of them, and become opponents to them.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: By no means! They shall soon deny their worship and shall become their adversaries instead.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: Nay, but they will deny their worship of them, and will become their adversaries.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: Nay, but they will deny their worship of them, and become opponents unto them.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: No Indeed! Soon they will disown their worship, and they will be their opponents.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: Indeed no! They will renounce their worship and turn against them.

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: No! Those "gods" will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents [on the Day of Judgement].

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: In fact, they can have no honor; their gods will renounce their worship of idols and will turn against them.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: By no means! They shall soon deny their worshipping them, and they shall be adversaries to them.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: But they shall reject their worship and turn against them.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: Instead, they shall reject their worship, and become adversaries against them.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Kalla sayakfuroona biAAibadatihim wayakoonoona AAalayhim diddan

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: Do you not see that We have set the devils against the infidels to rouse and instigate them?

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: Did you not see that We sent devils upon the disbelievers, so they excite them abundantly?

83.author: Arberry
83.text: Hast thou not seen how We sent the Satans against the unbelievers, to prick them?

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: Beholdest thou not that We have sent the satans upon the infidels inciting them by an incitement? *Chapter: 19

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: See you not that We have sent the Shayatin (devils) against the disbelievers to push them to do evil.

83.author: Itani
83.text: Have you not considered how We dispatch the devils against the disbelievers, exciting them with incitement?

83.author: Maududi
83.text: Do you not see that We have sent devils upon the unbelievers who greatly incite them (to oppose the Truth)?

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: See you not that We have sent the Shayatin against the disbelievers to push them to do evil.

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: Seest thou not that We have set the devils on the disbelievers to confound them with confusion?

83.author: Qarai
83.text: Have you not regarded that We unleash the devils upon the faithless to urge them vigorously?

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: Have you not seen how We send down to the unbelievers satans who prick them?

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them to [evil] with [constant] incitement?

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: Do you not realize that We have sent Satan to incite the unbelievers to sin.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: Do you not see that We have sent the Shaitans against the unbelievers, inciting them by incitement?

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: Do you not see that We have appointed devils to incite those who deny the truth to disobedience?

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: Seest thou not that We have set the Evil Ones on against the unbelievers, to incite them with fury?

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Alam tara anna arsalna alshshayateena AAala alkafireena taozzuhum azzan

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: So, do not be hasty with them. We are counting their number (of days).

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: So do not be impatient for them (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him); We are only completing their number.* (* The number of days left for them or their evil deeds.)

84.author: Arberry
84.text: So hasten thou not against them; We are only numbering for them a number.

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: So hasten thou not against them; We are only counting against them a counting. *Chapter: 19

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: So make no haste against them; We only count out to them a (limited) number (of the days of the life of this world and delay their term so that they may increase in evil and sins).

84.author: Itani
84.text: So do not hurry against them. We are counting for them a countdown.

84.author: Maududi
84.text: Therefore, do not hasten (in seeking a scourge against them). We are counting their days.

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: So make no haste against them; We only count out to them a (limited) number.

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: So make no haste against them (O Muhammad). We do but number unto them a sum (of days).

84.author: Qarai
84.text: So do not make haste against them; indeed We are counting for them, a counting [down].

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: Therefore, do not hasten (things) against them, for We count out to them a number,

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: So be not impatient over them. We only count out to them a [limited] number.

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: (Muhammad), exercise patience; We have given them respite only for an appointed time.

84.author: Shakir
84.text: Therefore be not in haste against them, We only number out to them a number (of days).

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: So take no hasty action against them; their days are numbered.

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: So make no haste against them, for We but count out to them a (limited) number (of days).

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Fala taAAjal AAalayhim innama naAAuddu lahum AAaddan

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: The day We shall usher the righteous before Ar-Rahman like envoys into the presence of a king,

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: On the day when We shall assemble the righteous towards the Most Gracious, as guests.

85.author: Arberry
85.text: On the day that We shall muster the godfearing to the All-merciful with pomp

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: On the Day whereon We shall gather the God-feaing unto the Com- passionate as an embassy. *Chapter: 19

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: The Day We shall gather the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2) unto the Most Beneficent (Allah), like a delegate (presented before a king for honour).

85.author: Itani
85.text: On the Day when We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful, as guests.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: The Day shall soon come when We shall bring together the God-fearing to the Most Compassionate Lord, as honoured guests;

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: The Day We shall gather those with Taqwa unto the Most Gracious (Allah), like a delegation.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: On the day when We shall gather the righteous unto the Beneficent, a goodly company.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: The day We shall gather the Godwary toward the All-beneficent, on mounts,

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: and on the Day when We will ceremonially gather the righteous to the Merciful,

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: On the Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: On the Day of Judgment, when the pious people will be brought into the presence of the Beneficent God as the guests of honor

85.author: Shakir
85.text: The day on which We will gather those who guard (against evil) to the Beneficent Allah to receive honors

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: The Day will surely come when We shall gather the God-fearing like [honoured] guests before the Compassionate God

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: The day We shall gather the righteous to (Allah) Most Gracious, like a band presented before a king for honours,

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: Yawma nahshuru almuttaqeena ila alrrahmani wafdan

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: And drive the wicked into Hell like cattle driven to water,

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: And drive the guilty towards hell, thirsty.

86.author: Arberry
86.text: and drive the evildoers into Gehenna herding,

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: And shall drive the culprits to Hell as a herd. *Chapter: 19

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: And We shall drive the Mujrimun (polytheists, sinners, criminals, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, etc.) to Hell, in a thirsty state (like a thirsty herd driven down to water),

86.author: Itani
86.text: And herd the sinners into hell, like animals to water.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: and We shall drive the guilty ones to Hell as a thirsty herd.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: And We shall drive the criminals to Hell, in a thirsty state.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: And drive the guilty unto hell, a weary herd,

86.author: Qarai
86.text: and drive the guilty as a thirsty herd towards hell,

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: and drive the evildoers as herds, into Gehenna

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: And will drive the criminals to Hell in thirst

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: and the criminals will be driven and thrown into hell,

86.author: Shakir
86.text: And We will drive the guilty to hell thirsty

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: and We shall drive the sinful like a thirsty herd into Hell.

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: And We shall drive the sinners to Hell, like thirsty cattle driven down to water,-

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: None will have power to intercede for them except one who obtains a promise from Ar-Rahman.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: People do not own the right to intercede, except those* who have made a covenant with the Most Gracious. (* The Holy Prophets and virtuous people will be given the permission to intercede. Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – will be the first to intercede.)

87.author: Arberry
87.text: having no power of intercession, save those who have taken with the All-merciful covenant.

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: They shall not own intercession, excepting those who have taken of the Compassionate a covenant. *Chapter: 19

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: None shall have the power of intercession, but such a one as has received permission (or promise) from the Most Beneficent (Allah).

87.author: Itani
87.text: They will have no power of intercession, except for someone who has an agreement with the Most Merciful.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: On that Day none will have the power to intercede for them except those who received a sanction from the Most Compassionate Lord.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: None shall have the power of intercession, but such a one as has received permission (or promise) from the Most Gracious (Allah).

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: They will have no power of intercession, save him who hath made a covenant with his Lord.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: no one will have the power to intercede [with Allah], except for him who has taken a covenant with the All-beneficent.

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: who have no power of intercession, except those who have taken a covenant with the Merciful.

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: None will have [power of] intercession except he who had taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: no one will be able to intercede for the others except those whom the Beneficent God has given authority.

87.author: Shakir
87.text: They shall not control intercession, save he who has made a covenant with the Beneficent Allah.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: No one will have power to intercede, except for those who have permission from the Lord of Mercy.

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: None shall have the power of intercession, but such a one as has received permission (or promise) from (Allah) Most Gracious.

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: La yamlikoona alshshafaAAata illa mani ittakhatha AAinda alrrahmani AAahdan

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: They say: "God has begotten a son."

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: And the disbelievers said, “The Most Gracious has chosen an offspring.”

88.author: Arberry
88.text: And they say, 'The All-merciful has taken unto Himself a son.

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: And they say: the Compassionate hath taken a son. *Chapter: 19

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: And they say: "The Most Beneficent (Allah) has begotten a son (or offspring or children) [as the Jews say: 'Uzair (Ezra) is the son of Allah, and the Christians say that He has begotten a son ['Iesa (Christ)], and the pagan Arabs say that He has begotten daughters (angels, etc.)]."

88.author: Itani
88.text: And they say, “The Most Merciful has begotten a son.”

88.author: Maududi
88.text: They claim: "The Most Compassionate Lord has taken a son to Himself."

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: And they say: "The Most Gracious (Allah) has begotten a son."

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: And they say: The Beneficent hath taken unto Himself a son.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: They say, ‘The All-beneficent has taken a son!’

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: And they say: 'The Merciful has taken a son'

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken [for Himself] a son."

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: They have said that the Beneficent God has given birth to a son.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: And they say: The Beneficent Allah has taken (to Himself) a son.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: They say, "The Gracious One has begotten a son."

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: They say: "(Allah) Most Gracious has begotten a son!"

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: You have uttered a grievous thing

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: You have indeed brought an extremely grave speech!

89.author: Arberry
89.text: You have indeed advanced something hideous!

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: Assuredly ye have brought a thing monstrous. *Chapter: 19

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: Indeed you have brought forth (said) a terrible evil thing.

89.author: Itani
89.text: You have come up with something monstrous.

89.author: Maududi
89.text: Surely you have made a monstrous statement.

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: Indeed you have brought forth (said) a thing Idda.

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: Assuredly ye utter a disastrous thing

89.author: Qarai
89.text: You have certainly advanced something hideous!

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: Surely, you have brought a monstrous thing!

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: You have done an atrocious thing.

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: This is certainly a monstrous lie!

89.author: Shakir
89.text: Certainly you have made an abominable assertion

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: Assuredly, you have uttered a monstrous falsehood:

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: Indeed ye have put forth a thing most monstrous!

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: Which would cleave the skies asunder, rend the earth, and split the mountains,

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: The heavens are close to being torn apart by it, and the earth being split asunder, and the mountains succumbing and falling down.

90.author: Arberry
90.text: The heavens are wellnigh rent of it and the earth split asunder, and the mountains wellnigh fall down crashing for

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: Well-nigh the heavens are rent thereat and the earth cleft in sunder and the mountains fall down in pieces. *Chapter: 19

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: Whereby the heavens are almost torn, and the earth is split asunder, and the mountains fall in ruins,

90.author: Itani
90.text: At which the heavens almost rupture, and the earth splits, and the mountains fall and crumble.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: It is such a monstrosity that heavens might well-nigh burst forth at it, the earth might be cleaved, and the mountains fall

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: Whereby the heavens are almost torn, and the earth is split asunder, and the mountains Hadda.

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: Whereby almost the heavens are torn, and the earth is split asunder and the mountains fall in ruins,

90.author: Qarai
90.text: The heavens are about to be rent apart at it, the earth to split open, and the mountains to collapse into bits,

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: Thereby the heavens are nearly torn and the earth split asunder, and mountains fall crashing down

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: The heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: This would almost cause the heavens to rent apart, the earth to cleave asunder and the mountains to crumble down in fragments,

90.author: Shakir
90.text: The heavens may almost be rent thereat, and the earth cleave asunder, and the mountains fall down in pieces,

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: the heavens might well-nigh burst thereat, and the earth break asunder, and the mountains fall down in pieces,

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: At it the skies are ready to burst, the earth to split asunder, and the mountains to fall down in utter ruin,

90.author: Transliteration
90.text: Takadu alssamawatu yatafattarna minhu watanshaqqu alardu watakhirru aljibalu haddan

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: For they have attributed a son to Ar-Rahman,

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: Due to their ascribing of an offspring to the Most Gracious.

91.author: Arberry
91.text: that they have attributed to the All-merciful a son;

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: That they should ascribe unto the Compassionate a son. *Chapter: 19

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: That they ascribe a son (or offspring or children) to the Most Beneficent (Allah).

91.author: Itani
91.text: Because they attribute a son to the Most Merciful.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: at their ascribing a son to the Most Compassionate Lord.

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: That they ascribe a son to the Most Gracious.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: That ye ascribe unto the Beneficent a son,

91.author: Qarai
91.text: that they should ascribe a son to the All-beneficent!

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: because they have ascribed a son to the Merciful.

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: That they attribute to the Most Merciful a son.

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: to ascribe a son to the Beneficent God.

91.author: Shakir
91.text: That they ascribe a son to the Beneficent Allah.

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: because they ascribe a son to the Gracious One.

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: That they should invoke a son for (Allah) Most Gracious.

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: When it does not behove the Merciful to have a son.

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: And it does not befit the Most Gracious to choose an offspring!

92.author: Arberry
92.text: and it behoves not the All-merciful to take a son.

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: And it behoveth not the Compassionate that he should take a son. *Chapter: 19

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: But it is not suitable for (the Majesty of) the Most Beneficent (Allah) that He should beget a son (or offspring or children).

92.author: Itani
92.text: It is not fitting for the Most Merciful to have a son.

92.author: Maududi
92.text: It does not befit the Most Compassionate Lord that He should take a son.

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: But it is not suitable for the Most Gracious that He should beget a son.

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: When it is not meet for (the Majesty of) the Beneficent that He should choose a son.

92.author: Qarai
92.text: It does not behoove the All-beneficent to take a son.

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: It is not for the Merciful to take a son!

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: And it is not appropriate for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: The Beneficent God is too Exalted to have a son.

92.author: Shakir
92.text: And it is not worthy of the Beneficent Allah that He should take (to Himself) a son.

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: It does not become the majesty of the Compassionate God to take to Himself a son:

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: For it is not consonant with the majesty of (Allah) Most Gracious that He should beget a son.

92.author: Transliteration
92.text: Wama yanbaghee lilrrahmani an yattakhitha waladan

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: There is no one in the heavens and the earth but comes before Ar-Rahman in all obedience.

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: All those who are in the heavens and the earth will come to the Most Gracious as His bondmen.

93.author: Arberry
93.text: None is there in the heavens and earth but he comes to the All-merciful as a servant;

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: None there is in the heavens and the earth but must come unto the Compassionate as a bondman. *Chapter: 19

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: There is none in the heavens and the earth but comes unto the Most Beneficent (Allah) as a slave.

93.author: Itani
93.text: There is none in the heavens and the earth but will come to the Most Merciful as a servant.

93.author: Maududi
93.text: There is no one in the heavens and the earth but he shall come to the Most Compassionate Lord as His servant.

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: There is none in the heavens and the earth but comes unto the Most Gracious as a slave.

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: There is none in the heavens and the earth but cometh unto the Beneficent as a slave.

93.author: Qarai
93.text: There is none in the heavens and the earth but he comes to the All-beneficent as a servant.

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: There is none in the heavens and earth except he comes to the Merciful as a worshiper.

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: There is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: All that is in the heavens and the earth will return to the Beneficent God as His submissive servants.

93.author: Shakir
93.text: There is no one in the heavens and the earth but will come to the Beneficent Allah as a servant.

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: there is none in the heavens or on the earth but shall return to the Merciful in utter submission,

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: Not one of the beings in the heavens and the earth but must come to (Allah) Most Gracious as a servant.

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: In kullu man fee alssamawati waalardi illa atee alrrahmani AAabdan

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: He has counted them and calculated their number.

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: He knows their number and has counted each one of them.

94.author: Arberry
94.text: He has indeed counted them, and He has numbered them exactly.

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: Assuredly He hath comprehanded them and counted them a full Counting. *Chapter: 19

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: Verily, He knows each one of them, and has counted them a full counting.

94.author: Itani
94.text: He has enumerated them, and counted them one by one.

94.author: Maududi
94.text: Verily He encompasses them and has counted them all.

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: Verily, He knows each one of them, and has counted them a full counting.

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: Verily He knoweth them and numbereth them with (right) numbering.

94.author: Qarai
94.text: Certainly He has counted them [all] and numbered them precisely,

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: He has counted them and exactly numbered all;

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: He has enumerated them and counted them a [full] counting.

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: He has counted and enumerated them one by one.

94.author: Shakir
94.text: Certainly He has a comprehensive knowledge of them and He has numbered them a (comprehensive) numbering.

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: He has counted them and numbered them precisely,

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: He does take an account of them (all), and hath numbered them (all) exactly.

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: Every one of them will come before Him all alone on the Day of Resurrection.

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: And each one of them will come before Him on the Day of Resurrection, alone.

95.author: Arberry
95.text: Every one of them shall come to Him upon the Day of Resurrection, all alone.

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: And each of them is to come to Him on the Day of Judgment alone. *Chapter: 19

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: And everyone of them will come to Him alone on the Day of Resurrection (without any helper, or protector or defender).

95.author: Itani
95.text: And each one of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.

95.author: Maududi
95.text: On the Day of Resurrection each one of these will come to Him singly.

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: And everyone of them will come to Him alone on the Day of Resurrection.

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: And each one of them will come unto Him on the Day of Resurrection, alone.

95.author: Qarai
95.text: and each of them will come to Him alone on the Day of Resurrection.

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: and each one shall come to Him on the Day of Resurrection, alone.

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: And all of them are coming to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: Everyone on the Day of Judgment will individually come into the presence of God.

95.author: Shakir
95.text: And every one of them will come to Him on the day of resurrection alone.

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: each one of them shall come to Him one by one on the Day of Judgement.

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: And everyone of them will come to Him singly on the Day of Judgment.

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Surely Ar-Rahman will show love for those who believe and do the right.

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Indeed those who believed and did good deeds – the Most Gracious will appoint love for them. (In the hearts of other believers.)

96.author: Arberry
96.text: Surely those who believe and do deeds of righteousness -- unto them the All-merciful shall assign love.

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Verily those who believe and do righteOUs works--anon the Compassionate shall appoint for them affection. *Chapter: 19

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Verily, those who believe [in the Oneness of Allah and in His Messenger (Muhammad SAW)] and work deeds of righteousness, the Most Beneficent (Allah) will bestow love for them (in the hearts of the believers).

96.author: Itani
96.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds, the Most Merciful will give them love.

96.author: Maududi
96.text: Indeed, the Most Compassionate Lord will soon create enduring love for those who believe and do righteous works.

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Verily, those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, the Most Gracious will bestow love for them.

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Lo! those who believe and do good works, the Beneficent will appoint for them love.

96.author: Qarai
96.text: Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—the All-beneficent will endear them [to His creation].

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds, the Merciful will assign for them love.

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them affection.

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: To the righteously striving believers God will grant love.

96.author: Shakir
96.text: Surely (as for) those who believe and do good deeds for them will Allah bring about love.

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: The Lord of Mercy will bestow affection upon those who believe and perform righteous deeds.

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: On those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, will (Allah) Most Gracious bestow love.

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati sayajAAalu lahumu alrrahmanu wuddan

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: So We have made this (Qur'an) easy in your tongue that you may give good news to those who take heed, and warn the people who are contentious.

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: We have therefore made this Qur’an easy upon your tongue, (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) for you to announce glad tidings with it to those who fear, and warn those who are quarrelsome.

97.author: Arberry
97.text: Now We have made it easy by thy tongue that thou mayest bear good tidings thereby to the godfearing, and warn a people stubborn.

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: So We have made it easy in thine tongue in order that thou mayest thereby give glad tidings unto the Godfearing and warn thereby a people contentious. *Chapter: 19

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: So We have made this (the Quran) easy in your own tongue (O Muhammad SAW), only that you may give glad tidings to the Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - See V. 2:2), and warn with it the Ludda (most quarrelsome) people.

97.author: Itani
97.text: We made it easy in your tongue, in order to deliver good news to the righteous, and to warn with it a hostile people.

97.author: Maududi
97.text: Therefore, We have revealed the Qur'an in your tongue and made it easy to understand that you may give glad tidings to the God-fearing and warn a contentious people.

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: So, We have made this easy in your own tongue, only that you may give glad tidings to those who have Taqwa, and warn with it the people that are Ludda.

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: And We make (this Scripture) easy in thy tongue, (O Muhammad) only that thou mayst bear good tidings therewith unto those who ward off (evil), and warn therewith the froward folk.

97.author: Qarai
97.text: Indeed We have made it simple in your language so that you may give good news thereby to the Godwary and warn with it a disputatious lot.

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: We have made it easy in your own tongue in order that you proclaim the glad tidings to the cautious and give warning to a stubborn nation.

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: So, [O Muhammad], We have only made Qur'an easy in the Arabic language that you may give good tidings thereby to the righteous and warn thereby a hostile people.

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: (Muhammad), We have given you the Book (the Quran) in your own language so that you could easily give the glad news to the pious ones and warn the quarrelsome ones.

97.author: Shakir
97.text: So We have only made it easy in your tongue that you may give good news thereby to those who guard (against evil) and warn thereby a vehemently contentious people.

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: We have made it [the Quran] easy, in your own language [Prophet], so that you may convey glad news to the righteous and give warning to a stubborn people.

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: So have We made the (Qur'an) easy in thine own tongue, that with it thou mayest give Glad Tidings to the righteous, and warnings to people given to contention.

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Fainnama yassarnahu bilisanika litubashshira bihi almuttaqeena watunthira bihi qawman luddan

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: How many generations have We laid low before them. Do you see any sign of them, or hear the least whisper of them?

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: And many a generation We did destroy before them; do you see any one of them or hear their faintest sound?

98.author: Arberry
98.text: And how many a generation We destroyed before them! Dost thou perceive so much as one of them, or hear of them a whisper?

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them! Perceivest thou of them anyone or hear of them a whisper? *Chapter: 20

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: And how many a generation before them have We destroyed! Can you (O Muhammad SAW) find a single one of them or hear even a whisper of them?

98.author: Itani
98.text: How many a generation have We destroyed before them? Can you feel a single one of them, or hear from them the slightest whisper?

98.author: Maududi
98.text: How numerous are the peoples that We destroyed before them! Do you now perceive any one of them, or hear even their whisper?

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: And how many a generation before them have We destroyed! Can you find a single one of them or hear even a whisper of them

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: And how many a generation before them have We destroyed! Canst thou (Muhammad) see a single man of them, or hear from them the slightest sound?

98.author: Qarai
98.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them! Can you descry any one of them, or hear from them so much as a murmur?

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them! Do you sense even one of them, or hear a whisper from them?

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: And how many have We destroyed before them of generations? Do you perceive of them anyone or hear from them a sound?

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: How many generations living before them did We destroy? Do you find anyone of them around or do you even hear any word from them?

98.author: Shakir
98.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them! Do you see any one of them or hear a sound of them?

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: How many generations We have destroyed before them! Can you find a single one of them alive now, or hear so much as a whisper from them?

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: But how many (countless) generations before them have We destroyed? Canst thou find a single one of them (now) or hear (so much as) a whisper of them?

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hal tuhissu minhum min ahadin aw tasmaAAu lahum rikzan
